       4              : /*-------------------------------------------------------------------------
       5              :  *
       6              :  * gram.y
       7              :  *        POSTGRESQL BISON rules/actions
       8              :  *
       9              :  * Portions Copyright (c) 1996-2020, PostgreSQL Global Development Group

      11              :  *
      12              :  *
      13              :  * IDENTIFICATION
      14              :  *        src/backend/parser/gram.y
      15              :  *
      16              :  * HISTORY
      17              :  *        AUTHOR                        DATE                    MAJOR EVENT
      18              :  *        Andrew Yu                     Sept, 1994              POSTQUEL to SQL conversion
      19              :  *        Andrew Yu                     Oct, 1994               lispy code conversion
      20              :  *
      21              :  * NOTES
      22              :  *        CAPITALS are used to represent terminal symbols.
      23              :  *        non-capitals are used to represent non-terminals.
      24              :  *
      25              :  *        In general, nothing in this file should initiate database accesses
      26              :  *        nor depend on changeable state (such as SET variables).  If you do
      27              :  *        database accesses, your code will fail when we have aborted the
      28              :  *        current transaction and are just parsing commands to find the next
      29              :  *        ROLLBACK or COMMIT.  If you make use of SET variables, then you
      30              :  *        will do the wrong thing in multi-query strings like this:
      31              :  *                      SET constraint_exclusion TO off; SELECT * FROM foo;
      32              :  *        because the entire string is parsed by gram.y before the SET gets
      33              :  *        executed.  Anything that depends on the database or changeable state
      34              :  *        should be handled during parse analysis so that it happens at the
      35              :  *        right time not the wrong time.
      36              :  *
      37              :  * WARNINGS
      38              :  *        If you use a list, make sure the datum is a node so that the printing
      39              :  *        routines work.
      40              :  *
      41              :  *        Sometimes we assign constants to makeStrings. Make sure we don't free
      42              :  *        those.
      43              :  *
      44              :  *-------------------------------------------------------------------------
      45              :  */

      69              : 
      70              : /*
      71              :  * Location tracking support --- simpler than bison's default, since we only
      72              :  * want to track the start position not the end position of each nonterminal.
      73              :  */
      74              : #define YYLLOC_DEFAULT(Current, Rhs, N) \
      75              :         do { \
      76              :                 if ((N) > 0) \
      77              :                         (Current) = (Rhs)[1]; \
      78              :                 else \
      79              :                         (Current) = (-1); \
      80              :         } while (0)
      81              : 
      82              : /*
      83              :  * The above macro assigns -1 (unknown) as the parse location of any
      84              :  * nonterminal that was reduced from an empty rule, or whose leftmost
      85              :  * component was reduced from an empty rule.  This is problematic
      86              :  * for nonterminals defined like
      87              :  *              OptFooList: / * EMPTY * / { ... } | OptFooList Foo { ... } ;
      88              :  * because we'll set -1 as the location during the first reduction and then
      89              :  * copy it during each subsequent reduction, leaving us with -1 for the
      90              :  * location even when the list is not empty.  To fix that, do this in the
      91              :  * action for the nonempty rule(s):
      92              :  *              if (@$ < 0) @$ = @2;
      93              :  * (Although we have many nonterminals that follow this pattern, we only
      94              :  * bother with fixing @$ like this when the nonterminal's parse location
      95              :  * is actually referenced in some rule.)
      96              :  *
      97              :  * A cleaner answer would be to make YYLLOC_DEFAULT scan all the Rhs
      98              :  * locations until it's found one that's not -1.  Then we'd get a correct
      99              :  * location for any nonterminal that isn't entirely empty.  But this way
     100              :  * would add overhead to every rule reduction, and so far there's not been
     101              :  * a compelling reason to pay that overhead.
     102              :  */
     103              : 
     104              : /*
     105              :  * Bison doesn't allocate anything that needs to live across parser calls,
     106              :  * so we can easily have it use palloc instead of malloc.  This prevents
     107              :  * memory leaks if we error out during parsing.  Note this only works with
     108              :  * bison >= 2.0.  However, in bison 1.875 the default is to use alloca()
     109              :  * if possible, so there's not really much problem anyhow, at least if
     110              :  * you're building with gcc.
     111              :  */
     112              : #define YYMALLOC palloc
     113              : #define YYFREE   pfree
     114              :

     117              : {
     118              :         GrantTargetType targtype;
     119              :         ObjectType      objtype;
     120              :         List       *objs;

     138              : /* ConstraintAttributeSpec yields an integer bitmask of these flags: */
     139              : #define CAS_NOT_DEFERRABLE                      0x01
     140              : #define CAS_DEFERRABLE                          0x02
     141              : #define CAS_INITIALLY_IMMEDIATE         0x04
     142              : #define CAS_INITIALLY_DEFERRED          0x08
     143              : #define CAS_NOT_VALID                           0x10
     144              : #define CAS_NO_INHERIT                          0x20

     582              : %type <node>      func_application func_expr_common_subexpr
     583              : %type <node>      func_expr func_expr_windowless
     584              : %type <node>      common_table_expr
     585              : %type <with>      with_clause opt_with_clause
     586              : %type <list>      cte_list
     587              : 
     588              : %type <list>      within_group_clause
     589              : %type <node>      filter_clause
     590              : %type <list>      window_clause window_definition_list opt_partition_clause
     591              : %type <windef>    window_definition over_clause window_specification
     592              :                                 opt_frame_clause frame_extent frame_bound
     593              : %type <ival>      opt_window_exclusion_clause
     594              : %type <str>               opt_existing_window_name
     595              : %type <boolean> opt_if_not_exists
     596              : %type <ival>      generated_when override_kind
     597              : %type <partspec>  PartitionSpec OptPartitionSpec
     598              : %type <partelem>  part_elem
     599              : %type <list>              part_params
     600              : %type <partboundspec> PartitionBoundSpec
     601              : %type <list>              hash_partbound
     602              : %type <defelt>            hash_partbound_elem
     603              : 
     604              : /*
     605              :  * Non-keyword token types.  These are hard-wired into the "flex" lexer.
     606              :  * They must be listed first so that their numeric codes do not depend on
     607              :  * the set of keywords.  PL/pgSQL depends on this so that it can share the
     608              :  * same lexer.  If you add/change tokens here, fix PL/pgSQL to match!
     609              :  *
     610              :  * UIDENT and USCONST are reduced to IDENT and SCONST in parser.c, so that
     611              :  * they need no productions here; but we must assign token codes to them.
     612              :  *
     613              :  * DOT_DOT is unused in the core SQL grammar, and so will always provoke
     614              :  * parse errors.  It is needed by PL/pgSQL.
     615              :  */
     616              : %token <str>      IDENT UIDENT FCONST SCONST USCONST BCONST XCONST Op
     617              : %token <ival>     ICONST PARAM
     618              : %token                  TYPECAST DOT_DOT COLON_EQUALS EQUALS_GREATER
     619              : %token                  LESS_EQUALS GREATER_EQUALS NOT_EQUALS
     620              : 
     621              : /*
     622              :  * If you want to make any keyword changes, update the keyword table in
     623              :  * src/include/parser/kwlist.h and add new keywords to the appropriate one
     624              :  * of the reserved-or-not-so-reserved keyword lists, below; search
     625              :  * this file for "Keyword category lists".
     626              :  */
     627              : 
     628              : /* ordinary key words in alphabetical order */
     629              : %token <keyword> ABORT_P ABSOLUTE_P ACCESS ACTION ADD_P ADMIN AFTER
     630              :         AGGREGATE ALL ALSO ALTER ALWAYS ANALYSE ANALYZE AND ANY ARRAY AS ASC
     631              :         ASSERTION ASSIGNMENT ASYMMETRIC AT ATTACH ATTRIBUTE AUTHORIZATION
     632              : 
     633              :         BACKWARD BEFORE BEGIN_P BETWEEN BIGINT BINARY BIT
     634              :         BOOLEAN_P BOTH BY
     635              : 
     636              :         CACHE CALL CALLED CASCADE CASCADED CASE CAST CATALOG_P CHAIN CHAR_P
     637              :         CHARACTER CHARACTERISTICS CHECK CHECKPOINT CLASS CLOSE
     638              :         CLUSTER COALESCE COLLATE COLLATION COLUMN COLUMNS COMMENT COMMENTS COMMIT
     639              :         COMMITTED CONCURRENTLY CONFIGURATION CONFLICT CONNECTION CONSTRAINT
     640              :         CONSTRAINTS CONTENT_P CONTINUE_P CONVERSION_P COPY COST CREATE
     641              :         CROSS CSV CUBE CURRENT_P
     642              :         CURRENT_CATALOG CURRENT_DATE CURRENT_ROLE CURRENT_SCHEMA
     643              :         CURRENT_TIME CURRENT_TIMESTAMP CURRENT_USER CURSOR CYCLE
     644              : 
     645              :         DATA_P DATABASE DAY_P DEALLOCATE DEC DECIMAL_P DECLARE DEFAULT DEFAULTS
     646              :         DEFERRABLE DEFERRED DEFINER DELETE_P DELIMITER DELIMITERS DEPENDS DESC
     647              :         DETACH DICTIONARY DISABLE_P DISCARD DISTINCT DO DOCUMENT_P DOMAIN_P
     648              :         DOUBLE_P DROP
     649              : 
     650              :         EACH ELSE ENABLE_P ENCODING ENCRYPTED END_P ENUM_P ESCAPE EVENT EXCEPT
     651              :         EXCLUDE EXCLUDING EXCLUSIVE EXECUTE EXISTS EXPLAIN EXPRESSION
     652              :         EXTENSION EXTERNAL EXTRACT
     653              : 
     654              :         FALSE_P FAMILY FETCH FILTER FIRST_P FLOAT_P FOLLOWING FOR
     655              :         FORCE FOREIGN FORWARD FREEZE FROM FULL FUNCTION FUNCTIONS
     656              : 
     657              :         GENERATED GLOBAL GRANT GRANTED GREATEST GROUP_P GROUPING GROUPS
     658              : 
     659              :         HANDLER HAVING HEADER_P HOLD HOUR_P
     660              : 
     661              :         IDENTITY_P IF_P ILIKE IMMEDIATE IMMUTABLE IMPLICIT_P IMPORT_P IN_P INCLUDE
     662              :         INCLUDING INCREMENT INDEX INDEXES INHERIT INHERITS INITIALLY INLINE_P
     663              :         INNER_P INOUT INPUT_P INSENSITIVE INSERT INSTEAD INT_P INTEGER
     664              :         INTERSECT INTERVAL INTO INVOKER IS ISNULL ISOLATION
     665              : 
     666              :         JOIN
     667              : 
     668              :         KEY
     669              : 
     670              :         LABEL LANGUAGE LARGE_P LAST_P LATERAL_P
     671              :         LEADING LEAKPROOF LEAST LEFT LEVEL LIKE LIMIT LISTEN LOAD LOCAL
     672              :         LOCALTIME LOCALTIMESTAMP LOCATION LOCK_P LOCKED LOGGED
     673              : 
     674              :         MAPPING MATCH MATERIALIZED MAXVALUE METHOD MINUTE_P MINVALUE MODE MONTH_P MOVE
     675              : 
     676              :         NAME_P NAMES NATIONAL NATURAL NCHAR NEW NEXT NFC NFD NFKC NFKD NO NONE
     677              :         NORMALIZE NORMALIZED
     678              :         NOT NOTHING NOTIFY NOTNULL NOWAIT NULL_P NULLIF
     679              :         NULLS_P NUMERIC
     680              : 
     681              :         OBJECT_P OF OFF OFFSET OIDS OLD ON ONLY OPERATOR OPTION OPTIONS OR
     682              :         ORDER ORDINALITY OTHERS OUT_P OUTER_P
     683              :         OVER OVERLAPS OVERLAY OVERRIDING OWNED OWNER
     684              : 
     685              :         PARALLEL PARSER PARTIAL PARTITION PASSING PASSWORD PLACING PLANS POLICY
     686              :         POSITION PRECEDING PRECISION PRESERVE PREPARE PREPARED PRIMARY
     687              :         PRIOR PRIVILEGES PROCEDURAL PROCEDURE PROCEDURES PROGRAM PUBLICATION
     688              : 
     689              :         QUOTE
     690              : 
     691              :         RANGE READ REAL REASSIGN RECHECK RECURSIVE REF_P REFERENCES REFERENCING
     692              :         REFRESH REINDEX RELATIVE_P RELEASE RENAME REPEATABLE REPLACE REPLICA
     693              :         RESET RESTART RESTRICT RETURNING RETURNS REVOKE RIGHT ROLE ROLLBACK ROLLUP
     694              :         ROUTINE ROUTINES ROW ROWS RULE
     695              : 
     696              :         SAVEPOINT SCHEMA SCHEMAS SCROLL SEARCH SECOND_P SECURITY SELECT SEQUENCE SEQUENCES
     697              :         SERIALIZABLE SERVER SESSION SESSION_USER SET SETS SETOF SHARE SHOW
     698              :         SIMILAR SIMPLE SKIP SMALLINT SNAPSHOT SOME SQL_P STABLE STANDALONE_P
     699              :         START STATEMENT STATISTICS STDIN STDOUT STORAGE STORED STRICT_P STRIP_P
     700              :         SUBSCRIPTION SUBSTRING SUPPORT SYMMETRIC SYSID SYSTEM_P
     701              : 
     702              :         TABLE TABLES TABLESAMPLE TABLESPACE TEMP TEMPLATE TEMPORARY TEXT_P THEN
     703              :         TIES TIME TIMESTAMP TO TRAILING TRANSACTION TRANSFORM
     704              :         TREAT TRIGGER TRIM TRUE_P
     705              :         TRUNCATE TRUSTED TYPE_P TYPES_P
     706              : 
     707              :         UESCAPE UNBOUNDED UNCOMMITTED UNENCRYPTED UNION UNIQUE UNKNOWN
     708              :         UNLISTEN UNLOGGED UNTIL UPDATE USER USING
     709              : 
     710              :         VACUUM VALID VALIDATE VALIDATOR VALUE_P VALUES VARCHAR VARIADIC VARYING
     711              :         VERBOSE VERSION_P VIEW VIEWS VOLATILE
     712              : 
     713              :         WHEN WHERE WHITESPACE_P WINDOW WITH WITHIN WITHOUT WORK WRAPPER WRITE
     714              : 
     715              :         XML_P XMLATTRIBUTES XMLCONCAT XMLELEMENT XMLEXISTS XMLFOREST XMLNAMESPACES
     716              :         XMLPARSE XMLPI XMLROOT XMLSERIALIZE XMLTABLE
     717              : 
     718              :         YEAR_P YES_P
     719              : 
     720              :         ZONE
     721              : 
     722              : /*
     723              :  * The grammar thinks these are keywords, but they are not in the kwlist.h
     724              :  * list and so can never be entered directly.  The filter in parser.c
     725              :  * creates these tokens when required (based on looking one token ahead).
     726              :  *
     727              :  * NOT_LA exists so that productions such as NOT LIKE can be given the same
     728              :  * precedence as LIKE; otherwise they'd effectively have the same precedence
     729              :  * as NOT, at least with respect to their left-hand subexpression.
     730              :  * NULLS_LA and WITH_LA are needed to make the grammar LALR(1).
     731              :  */
     732              : %token          NOT_LA NULLS_LA WITH_LA
     733              : 
     734              : 
     735              : /* Precedence: lowest to highest */
     736              : %nonassoc       SET                             /* see relation_expr_opt_alias */
     737              : %left           UNION EXCEPT
     738              : %left           INTERSECT
     739              : %left           OR
     740              : %left           AND
     741              : %right          NOT
     742              : %nonassoc       IS ISNULL NOTNULL       /* IS sets precedence for IS NULL, etc */
     743              : %nonassoc       '<' '>' '=' LESS_EQUALS GREATER_EQUALS NOT_EQUALS
     744              : %nonassoc       BETWEEN IN_P LIKE ILIKE SIMILAR NOT_LA
     745              : %nonassoc       ESCAPE                  /* ESCAPE must be just above LIKE/ILIKE/SIMILAR */
     746              : %left           POSTFIXOP               /* dummy for postfix Op rules */
     747              : /*
     748              :  * To support target_el without AS, we must give IDENT an explicit priority
     749              :  * between POSTFIXOP and Op.  We can safely assign the same priority to
     750              :  * various unreserved keywords as needed to resolve ambiguities (this can't
     751              :  * have any bad effects since obviously the keywords will still behave the
     752              :  * same as if they weren't keywords).  We need to do this:
     753              :  * for PARTITION, RANGE, ROWS, GROUPS to support opt_existing_window_name;
     754              :  * for RANGE, ROWS, GROUPS so that they can follow a_expr without creating
     755              :  * postfix-operator problems;
     756              :  * for GENERATED so that it can follow b_expr;
     757              :  * and for NULL so that it can follow b_expr in ColQualList without creating
     758              :  * postfix-operator problems.
     759              :  *
     760              :  * To support CUBE and ROLLUP in GROUP BY without reserving them, we give them
     761              :  * an explicit priority lower than '(', so that a rule with CUBE '(' will shift
     762              :  * rather than reducing a conflicting rule that takes CUBE as a function name.
     763              :  * Using the same precedence as IDENT seems right for the reasons given above.
     764              :  *
     765              :  * The frame_bound productions UNBOUNDED PRECEDING and UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
     766              :  * are even messier: since UNBOUNDED is an unreserved keyword (per spec!),
     767              :  * there is no principled way to distinguish these from the productions
     768              :  * a_expr PRECEDING/FOLLOWING.  We hack this up by giving UNBOUNDED slightly
     769              :  * lower precedence than PRECEDING and FOLLOWING.  At present this doesn't
     770              :  * appear to cause UNBOUNDED to be treated differently from other unreserved
     771              :  * keywords anywhere else in the grammar, but it's definitely risky.  We can
     772              :  * blame any funny behavior of UNBOUNDED on the SQL standard, though.
     773              :  */
     774              : %nonassoc       UNBOUNDED               /* ideally should have same precedence as IDENT */
     775              : %nonassoc       IDENT GENERATED NULL_P PARTITION RANGE ROWS GROUPS PRECEDING FOLLOWING CUBE ROLLUP
     776              : %left           Op OPERATOR             /* multi-character ops and user-defined operators */
     777              : %left           '+' '-'
     778              : %left           '*' '/' '%'
     779              : %left           '^'
     780              : /* Unary Operators */
     781              : %left           AT                              /* sets precedence for AT TIME ZONE */
     782              : %left           COLLATE
     783              : %right          UMINUS
     784              : %left           '[' ']'
     785              : %left           '(' ')'
     786              : %left           TYPECAST
     787              : %left           '.'
     788              : /*
     789              :  * These might seem to be low-precedence, but actually they are not part
     790              :  * of the arithmetic hierarchy at all in their use as JOIN operators.
     791              :  * We make them high-precedence to support their use as function names.
     792              :  * They wouldn't be given a precedence at all, were it not that we need
     793              :  * left-associativity among the JOIN rules themselves.
     794              :  */
     795              : %left           JOIN CROSS LEFT FULL RIGHT INNER_P NATURAL
     796              : /* kluge to keep xml_whitespace_option from causing shift/reduce conflicts */
     797              : %right          PRESERVE STRIP_P
     798              : 
     799              : %%
     800              : 
     801              : /*
     802              :  *      The target production for the whole parse.
     803              :  */
     804              : stmtblock:      stmtmulti
     805              :                         {
     806         1337 :                                 pg_yyget_extra(yyscanner)->parsetree = $1;
     807              :                                 (void) yynerrs;         /* suppress compiler warning */
     808              :                         }
     809              :                 ;
     810              : 
     811              : /*
     812              :  * At top level, we wrap each stmt with a RawStmt node carrying start location
     813              :  * and length of the stmt's text.  Notice that the start loc/len are driven
     814              :  * entirely from semicolon locations (@2).  It would seem natural to use
     815              :  * @1 or @3 to get the true start location of a stmt, but that doesn't work
     816              :  * for statements that can start with empty nonterminals (opt_with_clause is
     817              :  * the main offender here); as noted in the comments for YYLLOC_DEFAULT,
     818              :  * we'd get -1 for the location in such cases.
     819              :  * We also take care to discard empty statements entirely.
     820              :  */
     821              : stmtmulti:      stmtmulti ';' stmt
     822              :                                 {
     823         1400 :                                         if ($1 != NIL)
     824              :                                         {
     825              :                                                 /* update length of previous stmt */
     826         1400 :                                                 updateRawStmtEnd(llast_node(RawStmt, $1), @2);
     827              :                                         }
     828         1400 :                                         if ($3 != NULL)
     829          115 :                                                 $$ = lappend($1, makeRawStmt($3, @2 + 1));
     830              :                                         else
     831         1285 :                                                 $$ = $1;
     832              :                                 }
     833              :                         | stmt
     834              :                                 {
     835         1337 :                                         if ($1 != NULL)
     836         1337 :                                                 $$ = list_make1(makeRawStmt($1, 0));
     837              :                                         else
     838            0 :                                                 $$ = NIL;
     839              :                                 }
     840              :                 ;
     841              : 
     842              : stmt :
     843              :                         AlterEventTrigStmt
     844              :                         | AlterCollationStmt
     845              :                         | AlterDatabaseStmt
     846              :                         | AlterDatabaseSetStmt
     847              :                         | AlterDefaultPrivilegesStmt
     848              :                         | AlterDomainStmt
     849              :                         | AlterEnumStmt
     850              :                         | AlterExtensionStmt
     851              :                         | AlterExtensionContentsStmt
     852              :                         | AlterFdwStmt
     853              :                         | AlterForeignServerStmt
     854              :                         | AlterForeignTableStmt
     855              :                         | AlterFunctionStmt
     856              :                         | AlterGroupStmt
     857              :                         | AlterObjectDependsStmt
     858              :                         | AlterObjectSchemaStmt
     859              :                         | AlterOwnerStmt
     860              :                         | AlterOperatorStmt
     861              :                         | AlterTypeStmt
     862              :                         | AlterPolicyStmt
     863              :                         | AlterSeqStmt
     864              :                         | AlterSystemStmt
     865              :                         | AlterTableStmt
     866              :                         | AlterTblSpcStmt
     867              :                         | AlterCompositeTypeStmt
     868              :                         | AlterPublicationStmt
     869              :                         | AlterRoleSetStmt
     870              :                         | AlterRoleStmt
     871              :                         | AlterSubscriptionStmt
     872              :                         | AlterStatsStmt
     873              :                         | AlterTSConfigurationStmt
     874              :                         | AlterTSDictionaryStmt
     875              :                         | AlterUserMappingStmt
     876              :                         | AnalyzeStmt
     877              :                         | CallStmt
     878              :                         | CheckPointStmt
     879              :                         | ClosePortalStmt
     880              :                         | ClusterStmt
     881              :                         | CommentStmt
     882              :                         | ConstraintsSetStmt
     883              :                         | CopyStmt
     884              :                         | CreateAmStmt
     885              :                         | CreateAsStmt
     886              :                         | CreateAssertionStmt
     887              :                         | CreateCastStmt
     888              :                         | CreateConversionStmt
     889              :                         | CreateDomainStmt
     890              :                         | CreateExtensionStmt
     891              :                         | CreateFdwStmt
     892              :                         | CreateForeignServerStmt
     893              :                         | CreateForeignTableStmt
     894              :                         | CreateFunctionStmt
     895              :                         | CreateGroupStmt
     896              :                         | CreateMatViewStmt
     897              :                         | CreateOpClassStmt
     898              :                         | CreateOpFamilyStmt
     899              :                         | CreatePublicationStmt
     900              :                         | AlterOpFamilyStmt
     901              :                         | CreatePolicyStmt
     902              :                         | CreatePLangStmt
     903              :                         | CreateSchemaStmt
     904              :                         | CreateSeqStmt
     905              :                         | CreateStmt
     906              :                         | CreateSubscriptionStmt
     907              :                         | CreateStatsStmt
     908              :                         | CreateTableSpaceStmt
     909              :                         | CreateTransformStmt
     910              :                         | CreateTrigStmt
     911              :                         | CreateEventTrigStmt
     912              :                         | CreateRoleStmt
     913              :                         | CreateUserStmt
     914              :                         | CreateUserMappingStmt
     915              :                         | CreatedbStmt
     916              :                         | DeallocateStmt
     917              :                         | DeclareCursorStmt
     918              :                         | DefineStmt
     919              :                         | DeleteStmt
     920              :                         | DiscardStmt
     921              :                         | DoStmt
     922              :                         | DropCastStmt
     923              :                         | DropOpClassStmt
     924              :                         | DropOpFamilyStmt
     925              :                         | DropOwnedStmt
     926              :                         | DropPLangStmt
     927              :                         | DropStmt
     928              :                         | DropSubscriptionStmt
     929              :                         | DropTableSpaceStmt
     930              :                         | DropTransformStmt
     931              :                         | DropRoleStmt

     986              : /*****************************************************************************
     987              :  *

     991              : 
     992              : CreateRoleStmt:
     993              :                         CREATE ROLE RoleId opt_with OptRoleList
     994              :                                 {
     995            0 :                                         CreateRoleStmt *n = makeNode(CreateRoleStmt);
     996            0 :                                         n->stmt_type = ROLESTMT_ROLE;
     997            0 :                                         n->role = $3;
     998            0 :                                         n->options = $5;
     999            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    1000              :                                 }
    1001              :                 ;
    1002              : 
    1003              : 
    1004              : opt_with:       WITH                                                                    {}
    1005              :                         | WITH_LA                                                               {}
    1006              :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             {}
    1007              :                 ;
    1008              : 
    1009              : /*
    1010              :  * Options for CREATE ROLE and ALTER ROLE (also used by CREATE/ALTER USER
    1011              :  * for backwards compatibility).  Note: the only option required by SQL99
    1012              :  * is "WITH ADMIN name".
    1013              :  */
    1014              : OptRoleList:
    1015            0 :                         OptRoleList CreateOptRoleElem                   { $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
    1016            0 :                         | /* EMPTY */                                                   { $$ = NIL; }
    1017              :                 ;
    1018              : 
    1019              : AlterOptRoleList:
    1020            0 :                         AlterOptRoleList AlterOptRoleElem               { $$ = lappend($1, $2); }
    1021            0 :                         | /* EMPTY */                                                   { $$ = NIL; }
    1022              :                 ;
    1023              : 
    1024              : AlterOptRoleElem:
    1025              :                         PASSWORD Sconst
    1026              :                                 {
    1027            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("password",
    1028            0 :                                                                          (Node *)makeString($2), @1);
    1029              :                                 }
    1030              :                         | PASSWORD NULL_P
    1031              :                                 {
    1032            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("password", NULL, @1);
    1033              :                                 }
    1034              :                         | ENCRYPTED PASSWORD Sconst
    1035              :                                 {
    1036              :                                         /*
    1037              :                                          * These days, passwords are always stored in encrypted
    1038              :                                          * form, so there is no difference between PASSWORD and
    1039              :                                          * ENCRYPTED PASSWORD.
    1040              :                                          */
    1041            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("password",
    1042            0 :                                                                          (Node *)makeString($3), @1);
    1043              :                                 }
    1044              :                         | UNENCRYPTED PASSWORD Sconst
    1045              :                                 {
    1046            0 :                                         ereport(ERROR,
    1047              :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    1048              :                                                          errmsg("UNENCRYPTED PASSWORD is no longer supported"),
    1049              :                                                          errhint("Remove UNENCRYPTED to store the password in encrypted form instead."),
    1050              :                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
    1051              :                                 }
    1052              :                         | INHERIT
    1053              :                                 {
    1054            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("inherit", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1055              :                                 }
    1056              :                         | CONNECTION LIMIT SignedIconst
    1057              :                                 {
    1058            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("connectionlimit", (Node *)makeInteger($3), @1);
    1059              :                                 }
    1060              :                         | VALID UNTIL Sconst
    1061              :                                 {
    1062            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("validUntil", (Node *)makeString($3), @1);
    1063              :                                 }
    1064              :                 /*      Supported but not documented for roles, for use by ALTER GROUP. */
    1065              :                         | USER role_list
    1066              :                                 {
    1067            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("rolemembers", (Node *)$2, @1);
    1068              :                                 }
    1069              :                         | IDENT
    1070              :                                 {
    1071              :                                         /*
    1072              :                                          * We handle identifiers that aren't parser keywords with
    1073              :                                          * the following special-case codes, to avoid bloating the
    1074              :                                          * size of the main parser.
    1075              :                                          */
    1076            0 :                                         if (strcmp($1, "superuser") == 0)
    1077            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("superuser", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1078            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "nosuperuser") == 0)
    1079            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("superuser", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1080            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "createrole") == 0)
    1081            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("createrole", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1082            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "nocreaterole") == 0)
    1083            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("createrole", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1084            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "replication") == 0)
    1085            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("isreplication", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1086            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "noreplication") == 0)
    1087            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("isreplication", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1088            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "createdb") == 0)
    1089            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("createdb", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1090            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "nocreatedb") == 0)
    1091            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("createdb", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1092            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "login") == 0)
    1093            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("canlogin", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1094            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "nologin") == 0)
    1095            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("canlogin", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1096            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "bypassrls") == 0)
    1097            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("bypassrls", (Node *)makeInteger(true), @1);
    1098            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "nobypassrls") == 0)
    1099            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("bypassrls", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1100            0 :                                         else if (strcmp($1, "noinherit") == 0)
    1101              :                                         {
    1102              :                                                 /*
    1103              :                                                  * Note that INHERIT is a keyword, so it's handled by main parser, but
    1104              :                                                  * NOINHERIT is handled here.
    1105              :                                                  */
    1106            0 :                                                 $$ = makeDefElem("inherit", (Node *)makeInteger(false), @1);
    1107              :                                         }
    1108              :                                         else
    1109            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    1110              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    1111              :                                                                  errmsg("unrecognized role option \"%s\"", $1),
    1112              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
    1113              :                                 }
    1114              :                 ;

    1226              : /*****************************************************************************
    1227              :  *
    1228              :  * Drop a postgresql DBMS role
    1229              :  *
    1230              :  * XXX Ideally this would have CASCADE/RESTRICT options, but a role
    1231              :  * might own objects in multiple databases, and there is presently no way to
    1232              :  * implement cascading to other databases.  So we always behave as RESTRICT.
    1233              :  *****************************************************************************/
    1234              : 
    1235              : DropRoleStmt:
    1236              :                         DROP ROLE role_list
    1237              :                                 {

    1239            0 :                                         n->missing_ok = false;
    1240            0 :                                         n->roles = $3;
    1241            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    1242              :                                 }
    1243              :                         | DROP ROLE IF_P EXISTS role_list
    1244              :                                 {
    1245            0 :                                         DropRoleStmt *n = makeNode(DropRoleStmt);

    1247            0 :                                         n->roles = $5;
    1248            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    1249              :                                 }

    1328              : CreateSchemaStmt:
    1329              :                         CREATE SCHEMA OptSchemaName AUTHORIZATION RoleSpec OptSchemaEltList
    1330              :                                 {
    1331            0 :                                         CreateSchemaStmt *n = makeNode(CreateSchemaStmt);
    1332              :                                         /* One can omit the schema name or the authorization id. */
    1333            0 :                                         n->schemaname = $3;
    1334            0 :                                         n->authrole = $5;
    1335            0 :                                         n->schemaElts = $6;
    1336            0 :                                         n->if_not_exists = false;
    1337            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    1338              :                                 }
    1339              :                         | CREATE SCHEMA ColId OptSchemaEltList

    1343            1 :                                         n->schemaname = $3;

    1345            1 :                                         n->schemaElts = $4;
    1346            1 :                                         n->if_not_exists = false;
    1347            1 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    1348              :                                 }
    1349              :                         | CREATE SCHEMA IF_P NOT EXISTS OptSchemaName AUTHORIZATION RoleSpec OptSchemaEltList
    1350              :                                 {

    1352              :                                         /* schema name can be omitted here, too */
    1353            0 :                                         n->schemaname = $6;
    1354            0 :                                         n->authrole = $8;
    1355            0 :                                         if ($9 != NIL)
    1356            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    1357              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
    1358              :                                                                  errmsg("CREATE SCHEMA IF NOT EXISTS cannot include schema elements"),
    1359              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@9)));
    1360            0 :                                         n->schemaElts = $9;
    1361            0 :                                         n->if_not_exists = true;
    1362            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    1363              :                                 }

    1365              :                                 {

    1367              :                                         /* ...but not here */

    1374              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@7)));

    1388              :                                 {

    1396              : 
    1397              : /*
    1398              :  *      schema_stmt are the ones that can show up inside a CREATE SCHEMA
    1399              :  *      statement (in addition to by themselves).
    1400              :  */
    1401              : schema_stmt:
    1402              :                         CreateStmt
    1403              :                         | IndexStmt
    1404              :                         | CreateSeqStmt
    1405              :                         | CreateTrigStmt
    1406              :                         | GrantStmt
    1407              :                         | ViewStmt
    1408              :                 ;
    1409              : 
    1410              : 
    1411              : /*****************************************************************************
    1412              :  *
    1413              :  * Set PG internal variable
    1414              :  *        SET name TO 'var_value'
    1415              :  * Include SQL syntax (thomas 1997-10-22):
    1416              :  *        SET TIME ZONE 'var_value'
    1417              :  *
    1418              :  *****************************************************************************/
    1419              : 
    1420              : VariableSetStmt:
    1421              :                         SET set_rest
    1422              :                                 {
    1423           21 :                                         VariableSetStmt *n = $2;
    1424           21 :                                         n->is_local = false;
    1425           21 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
    1426              :                                 }
    1427              :                         | SET LOCAL set_rest
    1428              :                                 {
    1429            0 :                                         VariableSetStmt *n = $3;
    1430            0 :                                         n->is_local = true;
    1431            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
    1432              :                                 }
    1433              :                         | SET SESSION set_rest
    1434              :                                 {
    1435            0 :                                         VariableSetStmt *n = $3;
    1436            0 :                                         n->is_local = false;
    1437            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
    1438              :                                 }
    1439              :                 ;
    1440              : 
    1441              : set_rest:
    1442              :                         TRANSACTION transaction_mode_list
    1443              :                                 {
    1444            0 :                                         VariableSetStmt *n = makeNode(VariableSetStmt);
    1445            0 :                                         n->kind = VAR_SET_MULTI;
    1446            0 :                                         n->name = "TRANSACTION";
    1447            0 :                                         n->args = $2;
    1448            0 :                                         $$ = n;
    1449              :                                 }
    1450              :                         | SESSION CHARACTERISTICS AS TRANSACTION transaction_mode_list
    1451              :                                 {
    1452            0 :                                         VariableSetStmt *n = makeNode(VariableSetStmt);
    1453            0 :                                         n->kind = VAR_SET_MULTI;
    1454            0 :                                         n->name = "SESSION CHARACTERISTICS";

    1584           31 : var_name:       ColId                                                           { $$ = $1; }
    1585              :                         | var_name '.' ColId
    1586            0 :                                 { $$ = psprintf("%s.%s", $1, $3); }
    1587              :                 ;
    1588              : 
    1589           21 : var_list:       var_value                                                               { $$ = list_make1($1); }
    1590            0 :                         | var_list ',' var_value                                { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
    1591              :                 ;
    1592              : 
    1593              : var_value:      opt_boolean_or_string
    1594           21 :                                 { $$ = makeStringConst($1, @1); }
    1595              :                         | NumericOnly
    1596            0 :                                 { $$ = makeAConst($1, @1); }
    1597              :                 ;
    1598              : 
    1599            0 : iso_level:      READ UNCOMMITTED                                                { $$ = "read uncommitted"; }
    1600            0 :                         | READ COMMITTED                                                { $$ = "read committed"; }
    1601            1 :                         | REPEATABLE READ                                               { $$ = "repeatable read"; }
    1602            0 :                         | SERIALIZABLE                                                  { $$ = "serializable"; }
    1603              :                 ;
    1604              : 
    1605              : opt_boolean_or_string:
    1606            1 :                         TRUE_P                                                                  { $$ = "true"; }
    1607            1 :                         | FALSE_P                                                               { $$ = "false"; }
    1608            5 :                         | ON                                                                    { $$ = "on"; }
    1609              :                         /*
    1610              :                          * OFF is also accepted as a boolean value, but is handled by
    1611              :                          * the NonReservedWord rule.  The action for booleans and strings
    1612              :                          * is the same, so we don't need to distinguish them here.
    1613              :                          */
    1614           25 :                         | NonReservedWord_or_Sconst                             { $$ = $1; }
    1615              :                 ;
    1616              : 
    1617              : /* Timezone values can be:
    1618              :  * - a string such as 'pst8pdt'
    1619              :  * - an identifier such as "pst8pdt"
    1620              :  * - an integer or floating point number
    1621              :  * - a time interval per SQL99
    1622              :  * ColId gives reduce/reduce errors against ConstInterval and LOCAL,
    1623              :  * so use IDENT (meaning we reject anything that is a key word).
    1624              :  */
    1625              : zone_value:
    1626              :                         Sconst
    1627              :                                 {
    1628            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConst($1, @1);
    1629              :                                 }
    1630              :                         | IDENT
    1631              :                                 {
    1632            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConst($1, @1);
    1633              :                                 }
    1634              :                         | ConstInterval Sconst opt_interval
    1635              :                                 {
    1636            0 :                                         TypeName *t = $1;
    1637            0 :                                         if ($3 != NIL)
    1638              :                                         {
    1639            0 :                                                 A_Const *n = (A_Const *) linitial($3);
    1640            0 :                                                 if ((n->val.val.ival & ~(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) | INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE))) != 0)
    1641            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
    1642              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    1643              :                                                                          errmsg("time zone interval must be HOUR or HOUR TO MINUTE"),
    1644              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@3)));
    1645              :                                         }
    1646            0 :                                         t->typmods = $3;
    1647            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);
    1648              :                                 }
    1649              :                         | ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')' Sconst
    1650              :                                 {
    1651            0 :                                         TypeName *t = $1;
    1652            0 :                                         t->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),
    1653              :                                                                                         makeIntConst($3, @3));
    1654            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($5, @5, t);
    1655              :                                 }
    1656            0 :                         | NumericOnly                                                   { $$ = makeAConst($1, @1); }
    1657            0 :                         | DEFAULT                                                               { $$ = NULL; }
    1658            0 :                         | LOCAL                                                                 { $$ = NULL; }
    1659              :                 ;

    1663            0 :                         | DEFAULT                                                               { $$ = NULL; }
    1664            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NULL; }

    2708            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,

    2720              : PartitionBoundSpec:
    2721              :                         /* a HASH partition */
    2722              :                         FOR VALUES WITH '(' hash_partbound ')'
    2723              :                                 {
    2724              :                                         ListCell   *lc;
    2725            0 :                                         PartitionBoundSpec *n = makeNode(PartitionBoundSpec);
    2726              : 
    2727            0 :                                         n->strategy = PARTITION_STRATEGY_HASH;
    2728            0 :                                         n->modulus = n->remainder = -1;
    2729              : 
    2730            0 :                                         foreach (lc, $5)
    2731              :                                         {
    2732            0 :                                                 DefElem    *opt = lfirst_node(DefElem, lc);
    2733              : 
    2734            0 :                                                 if (strcmp(opt->defname, "modulus") == 0)
    2735              :                                                 {
    2736            0 :                                                         if (n->modulus != -1)
    2737            0 :                                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    2738              :                                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
    2739              :                                                                                  errmsg("modulus for hash partition provided more than once"),
    2740              :                                                                                  parser_errposition(opt->location)));
    2741            0 :                                                         n->modulus = defGetInt32(opt);
    2742              :                                                 }
    2743            0 :                                                 else if (strcmp(opt->defname, "remainder") == 0)
    2744              :                                                 {
    2745            0 :                                                         if (n->remainder != -1)
    2746            0 :                                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    2747              :                                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
    2748              :                                                                                  errmsg("remainder for hash partition provided more than once"),
    2749              :                                                                                  parser_errposition(opt->location)));
    2750            0 :                                                         n->remainder = defGetInt32(opt);
    2751              :                                                 }
    2752              :                                                 else
    2753            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
    2754              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    2755              :                                                                          errmsg("unrecognized hash partition bound specification \"%s\"",
    2756              :                                                                                         opt->defname),
    2757              :                                                                          parser_errposition(opt->location)));
    2758              :                                         }
    2759              : 
    2760            0 :                                         if (n->modulus == -1)
    2761            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    2762              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    2763              :                                                                  errmsg("modulus for hash partition must be specified")));
    2764            0 :                                         if (n->remainder == -1)
    2765            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    2766              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    2767              :                                                                  errmsg("remainder for hash partition must be specified")));
    2768              : 
    2769            0 :                                         n->location = @3;
    2770              : 
    2771            0 :                                         $$ = n;
    2772              :                                 }
    2773              : 
    2774              :                         /* a LIST partition */
    2775              :                         | FOR VALUES IN_P '(' expr_list ')'
    2776              :                                 {
    2777            5 :                                         PartitionBoundSpec *n = makeNode(PartitionBoundSpec);
    2778              : 
    2779            5 :                                         n->strategy = PARTITION_STRATEGY_LIST;
    2780            5 :                                         n->is_default = false;
    2781            5 :                                         n->listdatums = $5;
    2782            5 :                                         n->location = @3;
    2783              : 
    2784            5 :                                         $$ = n;
    2785              :                                 }
    2786              : 
    2787              :                         /* a RANGE partition */

    2789              :                                 {

    2791              :

    2796           28 :                                         n->location = @3;
    2797              : 
    2798           28 :                                         $$ = n;
    2799              :                                 }

    2809            0 :                                         $$ = n;
    2810              :                                 }

    2831              : /*****************************************************************************

    2838              : AlterCompositeTypeStmt:

    2840              :                                 {

    2842              :

    2845            0 :                                         n->cmds = $4;
    2846            0 :                                         n->relkind = OBJECT_TYPE;
    2847            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    2848              :                                 }
    2849              :                         ;

    2873            0 :                                         n->missing_ok = true;
    2874            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    2875              :                                 }
    2876              :                         /* ALTER TYPE <name> DROP ATTRIBUTE <attname> [RESTRICT|CASCADE] */

    2885              :                                 }

    2927              : 
    2928              : /*****************************************************************************
    2929              :  *
    2930              :  *              QUERY :
    2931              :  *                              COPY relname [(columnList)] FROM/TO file [WITH] [(options)]
    2932              :  *                              COPY ( query ) TO file  [WITH] [(options)]
    2933              :  *
    2934              :  *                              where 'query' can be one of:
    2935              :  *                              { SELECT | UPDATE | INSERT | DELETE }
    2936              :  *
    2937              :  *                              and 'file' can be one of:
    2938              :  *                              { PROGRAM 'command' | STDIN | STDOUT | 'filename' }
    2939              :  *
    2940              :  *                              In the preferred syntax the options are comma-separated
    2941              :  *                              and use generic identifiers instead of keywords.  The pre-9.0
    2942              :  *                              syntax had a hard-wired, space-separated set of options.
    2943              :  *
    2944              :  *                              Really old syntax, from versions 7.2 and prior:
    2945              :  *                              COPY [ BINARY ] table FROM/TO file
    2946              :  *                                      [ [ USING ] DELIMITERS 'delimiter' ] ]
    2947              :  *                                      [ WITH NULL AS 'null string' ]
    2948              :  *                              This option placement is not supported with COPY (query...).
    2949              :  *
    2950              :  *****************************************************************************/
    2951              : 
    2952              : CopyStmt:       COPY opt_binary qualified_name opt_column_list
    2953              :                         copy_from opt_program copy_file_name copy_delimiter opt_with
    2954              :                         copy_options where_clause
    2955              :                                 {
    2956            1 :                                         CopyStmt *n = makeNode(CopyStmt);
    2957            1 :                                         n->relation = $3;
    2958            1 :                                         n->query = NULL;
    2959            1 :                                         n->attlist = $4;
    2960            1 :                                         n->is_from = $5;
    2961            1 :                                         n->is_program = $6;
    2962            1 :                                         n->filename = $7;
    2963            1 :                                         n->whereClause = $11;
    2964              : 
    2965            1 :                                         if (n->is_program && n->filename == NULL)
    2966            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    2967              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    2968              :                                                                  errmsg("STDIN/STDOUT not allowed with PROGRAM"),
    2969              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@8)));
    2970              : 
    2971            1 :                                         if (!n->is_from && n->whereClause != NULL)
    2972            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    2973              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    2974              :                                                                  errmsg("WHERE clause not allowed with COPY TO"),
    2975              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@11)));
    2976              : 
    2977            1 :                                         n->options = NIL;
    2978              :                                         /* Concatenate user-supplied flags */
    2979            1 :                                         if ($2)
    2980            0 :                                                 n->options = lappend(n->options, $2);
    2981            1 :                                         if ($8)
    2982            0 :                                                 n->options = lappend(n->options, $8);
    2983            1 :                                         if ($10)
    2984            0 :                                                 n->options = list_concat(n->options, $10);
    2985            1 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    2986              :                                 }
    2987              :                         | COPY '(' PreparableStmt ')' TO opt_program copy_file_name opt_with copy_options
    2988              :                                 {
    2989            0 :                                         CopyStmt *n = makeNode(CopyStmt);
    2990            0 :                                         n->relation = NULL;
    2991            0 :                                         n->query = $3;
    2992            0 :                                         n->attlist = NIL;
    2993            0 :                                         n->is_from = false;
    2994            0 :                                         n->is_program = $6;
    2995            0 :                                         n->filename = $7;
    2996            0 :                                         n->options = $9;
    2997              : 
    2998            0 :                                         if (n->is_program && n->filename == NULL)
    2999            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    3000              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    3001              :                                                                  errmsg("STDIN/STDOUT not allowed with PROGRAM"),
    3002              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@5)));
    3003              : 
    3004            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    3005              :                                 }
    3006              :                 ;
    3007              : 
    3008              : copy_from:
    3009            1 :                         FROM                                                                    { $$ = true; }
    3010            0 :                         | TO                                                                    { $$ = false; }
    3011              :                 ;
    3012              : 
    3013              : opt_program:
    3014            0 :                         PROGRAM                                                                 { $$ = true; }
    3015            1 :                         | /* EMPTY */                                                   { $$ = false; }
    3016              :                 ;
    3017              : 
    3018              : /*
    3019              :  * copy_file_name NULL indicates stdio is used. Whether stdin or stdout is
    3020              :  * used depends on the direction. (It really doesn't make sense to copy from
    3021              :  * stdout. We silently correct the "typo".)            - AY 9/94
    3022              :  */
    3023              : copy_file_name:
    3024            1 :                         Sconst                                                                  { $$ = $1; }
    3025            0 :                         | STDIN                                                                 { $$ = NULL; }
    3026            0 :                         | STDOUT                                                                { $$ = NULL; }
    3027              :                 ;

    3029            1 : copy_options: copy_opt_list                                                     { $$ = $1; }

    3031              :                 ;
    3032              : 
    3033              : /* old COPY option syntax */

    3036            1 :                         | /* EMPTY */                                                   { $$ = NIL; }
    3037              :                 ;

    3039              : copy_opt_item:

    3287              : 
    3288              : /*
    3289              :  * Redundancy here is needed to avoid shift/reduce conflicts,
    3290              :  * since TEMP is not a reserved word.  See also OptTempTableName.
    3291              :  *
    3292              :  * NOTE: we accept both GLOBAL and LOCAL options.  They currently do nothing,
    3293              :  * but future versions might consider GLOBAL to request SQL-spec-compliant
    3294              :  * temp table behavior, so warn about that.  Since we have no modules the
    3295              :  * LOCAL keyword is really meaningless; furthermore, some other products
    3296              :  * implement LOCAL as meaning the same as our default temp table behavior,
    3297              :  * so we'll probably continue to treat LOCAL as a noise word.
    3298              :  */
    3299            0 : OptTemp:        TEMPORARY                                       { $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP; }
    3300            1 :                         | TEMP                                          { $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP; }
    3301            0 :                         | LOCAL TEMPORARY                       { $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP; }
    3302            0 :                         | LOCAL TEMP                            { $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP; }
    3303              :                         | GLOBAL TEMPORARY
    3304              :                                 {
    3305            0 :                                         ereport(WARNING,
    3306              :                                                         (errmsg("GLOBAL is deprecated in temporary table creation"),
    3307              :                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
    3308            0 :                                         $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
    3309              :                                 }
    3310              :                         | GLOBAL TEMP
    3311              :                                 {
    3312            0 :                                         ereport(WARNING,
    3313              :                                                         (errmsg("GLOBAL is deprecated in temporary table creation"),
    3314              :                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
    3315            0 :                                         $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_TEMP;
    3316              :                                 }
    3317            1 :                         | UNLOGGED                                      { $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_UNLOGGED; }
    3318          291 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                     { $$ = RELPERSISTENCE_PERMANENT; }
    3319              :                 ;
    3320              :

    3438              :                         | COLLATE any_name
    3439              :                                 {

    3444              :                                          */
    3445            2 :                                         CollateClause *n = makeNode(CollateClause);

    3452              : 
    3453              : /* DEFAULT NULL is already the default for Postgres.
    3454              :  * But define it here and carry it forward into the system
    3455              :  * to make it explicit.
    3456              :  * - thomas 1998-09-13
    3457              :  *
    3458              :  * WITH NULL and NULL are not SQL-standard syntax elements,
    3459              :  * so leave them out. Use DEFAULT NULL to explicitly indicate
    3460              :  * that a column may have that value. WITH NULL leads to
    3461              :  * shift/reduce conflicts with WITH TIME ZONE anyway.
    3462              :  * - thomas 1999-01-08
    3463              :  *
    3464              :  * DEFAULT expression must be b_expr not a_expr to prevent shift/reduce
    3465              :  * conflict on NOT (since NOT might start a subsequent NOT NULL constraint,
    3466              :  * or be part of a_expr NOT LIKE or similar constructs).
    3467              :  */
    3468              : ColConstraintElem:
    3469              :                         NOT NULL_P
    3470              :                                 {
    3471           18 :                                         Constraint *n = makeNode(Constraint);
    3472           18 :                                         n->contype = CONSTR_NOTNULL;
    3473           18 :                                         n->location = @1;
    3474           18 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
    3475              :                                 }
    3476              :                         | NULL_P

    3527              :                                 {

    3536              :                                 {

    3544              :                                         /*
    3545              :                                          * Can't do this in the grammar because of shift/reduce
    3546              :                                          * conflicts.  (IDENTITY allows both ALWAYS and BY
    3547              :                                          * DEFAULT, but generated columns only allow ALWAYS.)  We
    3548              :                                          * can also give a more useful error message and location.
    3549              :                                          */
    3550            0 :                                         if ($2 != ATTRIBUTE_IDENTITY_ALWAYS)
    3551            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
    3552              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
    3553              :                                                                  errmsg("for a generated column, GENERATED ALWAYS must be specified"),

    3556            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;

    3564            8 :                                         n->fk_attrs                  = NIL;
    3565            8 :                                         n->pk_attrs                  = $3;

    3568            8 :                                         n->fk_del_action     = (char) ($5 & 0xFF);
    3569            8 :                                         n->skip_validation  = false;

    3571            8 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
