   10315              : /*****************************************************************************
   10316              :  *
   10317              :  *              DROP DATABASE [ IF EXISTS ] dbname [ [ WITH ] ( options ) ]
   10318              :  *
   10319              :  * This is implicitly CASCADE, no need for drop behavior
   10320              :  *****************************************************************************/
   10321              : 
   10322              : DropdbStmt: DROP DATABASE database_name
   10323              :                                 {
   10324            0 :                                         DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
   10325            0 :                                         n->dbname = $3;
   10326            0 :                                         n->missing_ok = false;
   10327            0 :                                         n->options = NULL;
   10328            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   10329              :                                 }
   10330              :                         | DROP DATABASE IF_P EXISTS database_name
   10331              :                                 {
   10332            0 :                                         DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
   10333            0 :                                         n->dbname = $5;
   10334            0 :                                         n->missing_ok = true;
   10335            0 :                                         n->options = NULL;
   10336            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   10337              :                                 }
   10338              :                         | DROP DATABASE database_name opt_with '(' drop_option_list ')'
   10339              :                                 {
   10340            0 :                                         DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
   10341            0 :                                         n->dbname = $3;
   10342            0 :                                         n->missing_ok = false;
   10343            0 :                                         n->options = $6;
   10344            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   10345              :                                 }
   10346              :                         | DROP DATABASE IF_P EXISTS database_name opt_with '(' drop_option_list ')'
   10347              :                                 {
   10348            0 :                                         DropdbStmt *n = makeNode(DropdbStmt);
   10349            0 :                                         n->dbname = $5;
   10350            0 :                                         n->missing_ok = true;
   10351            0 :                                         n->options = $8;
   10352            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   10353              :                                 }
   10354              :                 ;
   10355              : 
   10356              : drop_option_list:
   10357              :                         drop_option
   10358              :                                 {
   10359            0 :                                         $$ = list_make1((Node *) $1);
   10360              :                                 }
   10361              :                         | drop_option_list ',' drop_option
   10362              :                                 {
   10363            0 :                                         $$ = lappend($1, (Node *) $3);
   10364              :                                 }
   10365              :                 ;
   10366              : 
   10367              : /*
   10368              :  * Currently only the FORCE option is supported, but the syntax is designed
   10369              :  * to be extensible so that we can add more options in the future if required.
   10370              :  */
   10371              : drop_option:
   10372              :                         FORCE
   10373              :                                 {
   10374            0 :                                         $$ = makeDefElem("force", NULL, @1);
   10375              :                                 }
   10376              :                 ;

   10587              : 
   10588              : /* Use this if TIME or ORDINALITY after WITH should be taken as an identifier */
   10589              : any_with:       WITH                                                                    {}

   10593              : 
   10594              : /*****************************************************************************
   10595              :  *
   10596              :  * Manipulate a conversion
   10597              :  *
   10598              :  *              CREATE [DEFAULT] CONVERSION <conversion_name>

   11007              : InsertStmt:
   11008              :                         opt_with_clause INSERT INTO insert_target insert_rest
   11009              :                         opt_on_conflict returning_clause
   11010              :                                 {
   11011          171 :                                         $5->relation = $4;

   11017              :                 ;
   11018              : 
   11019              : /*
   11020              :  * Can't easily make AS optional here, because VALUES in insert_rest would
   11021              :  * have a shift/reduce conflict with VALUES as an optional alias.  We could
   11022              :  * easily allow unreserved_keywords as optional aliases, but that'd be an odd
   11023              :  * divergence from other places.  So just require AS for now.
   11024              :  */
   11025              : insert_target:
   11026              :                         qualified_name
   11027              :                                 {
   11028          171 :                                         $$ = $1;
   11029              :                                 }
   11030              :                         | qualified_name AS ColId

   11034              :                                 }

   11123              :                                 {

   11125            0 :                                         $$->indexElems = $2;
   11126            0 :                                         $$->whereClause = $4;
   11127            0 :                                         $$->conname = NULL;
   11128            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   11129              :                                 }
   11130              :                         |
   11131              :                         ON CONSTRAINT name
   11132              :                                 {
   11133            0 :                                         $$ = makeNode(InferClause);
   11134            0 :                                         $$->indexElems = NIL;
   11135            0 :                                         $$->whereClause = NULL;
   11136            0 :                                         $$->conname = $3;
   11137            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   11138              :                                 }
   11139              :                         | /*EMPTY*/
   11140              :                                 {
   11141            0 :                                         $$ = NULL;
   11142              :                                 }
   11143              :                 ;
   11144              : 
   11145              : returning_clause:
   11146            1 :                         RETURNING target_list           { $$ = $2; }
   11147          196 :                         | /* EMPTY */                           { $$ = NIL; }
   11148              :                 ;
   11149              : 
   11150              : 
   11151              : /*****************************************************************************
   11152              :  *
   11153              :  *              QUERY:
   11154              :  *                              DELETE STATEMENTS
   11155              :  *
   11156              :  *****************************************************************************/
   11157              : 
   11158              : DeleteStmt: opt_with_clause DELETE_P FROM relation_expr_opt_alias
   11159              :                         using_clause where_or_current_clause returning_clause
   11160              :                                 {
   11161            8 :                                         DeleteStmt *n = makeNode(DeleteStmt);
   11162            8 :                                         n->relation = $4;
   11163            8 :                                         n->usingClause = $5;
   11164            8 :                                         n->whereClause = $6;
   11165            8 :                                         n->returningList = $7;
   11166            8 :                                         n->withClause = $1;
   11167            8 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11168              :                                 }
   11169              :                 ;
   11170              : 
   11171              : using_clause:
   11172            0 :                                 USING from_list                                         { $$ = $2; }
   11173            8 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NIL; }
   11174              :                 ;
   11175              : 
   11176              : 
   11177              : /*****************************************************************************
   11178              :  *
   11179              :  *              QUERY:
   11180              :  *                              LOCK TABLE
   11181              :  *
   11182              :  *****************************************************************************/
   11183              : 
   11184              : LockStmt:       LOCK_P opt_table relation_expr_list opt_lock opt_nowait
   11185              :                                 {
   11186            0 :                                         LockStmt *n = makeNode(LockStmt);
   11187              : 
   11188            0 :                                         n->relations = $3;
   11189            0 :                                         n->mode = $4;
   11190            0 :                                         n->nowait = $5;
   11191            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11192              :                                 }
   11193              :                 ;
   11194              : 
   11195            0 : opt_lock:       IN_P lock_type MODE                             { $$ = $2; }
   11196            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                             { $$ = AccessExclusiveLock; }
   11197              :                 ;
   11198              : 
   11199            0 : lock_type:      ACCESS SHARE                                    { $$ = AccessShareLock; }
   11200            0 :                         | ROW SHARE                                             { $$ = RowShareLock; }
   11201            0 :                         | ROW EXCLUSIVE                                 { $$ = RowExclusiveLock; }
   11202            0 :                         | SHARE UPDATE EXCLUSIVE                { $$ = ShareUpdateExclusiveLock; }
   11203            0 :                         | SHARE                                                 { $$ = ShareLock; }
   11204            0 :                         | SHARE ROW EXCLUSIVE                   { $$ = ShareRowExclusiveLock; }
   11205            0 :                         | EXCLUSIVE                                             { $$ = ExclusiveLock; }
   11206            0 :                         | ACCESS EXCLUSIVE                              { $$ = AccessExclusiveLock; }
   11207              :                 ;
   11208              : 
   11209            0 : opt_nowait:     NOWAIT                                                  { $$ = true; }
   11210            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                             { $$ = false; }
   11211              :                 ;
   11212              : 
   11213              : opt_nowait_or_skip:
   11214            0 :                         NOWAIT                                                  { $$ = LockWaitError; }
   11215            0 :                         | SKIP LOCKED                                   { $$ = LockWaitSkip; }
   11216           12 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                             { $$ = LockWaitBlock; }
   11217              :                 ;
   11218              : 
   11219              : 
   11220              : /*****************************************************************************
   11221              :  *
   11222              :  *              QUERY:
   11223              :  *                              UpdateStmt (UPDATE)
   11224              :  *
   11225              :  *****************************************************************************/
   11226              : 
   11227              : UpdateStmt: opt_with_clause UPDATE relation_expr_opt_alias
   11228              :                         SET set_clause_list
   11229              :                         from_clause
   11230              :                         where_or_current_clause
   11231              :                         returning_clause
   11232              :                                 {
   11233           18 :                                         UpdateStmt *n = makeNode(UpdateStmt);
   11234           18 :                                         n->relation = $3;
   11235           18 :                                         n->targetList = $5;
   11236           18 :                                         n->fromClause = $6;
   11237           18 :                                         n->whereClause = $7;
   11238           18 :                                         n->returningList = $8;
   11239           18 :                                         n->withClause = $1;
   11240           18 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11241              :                                 }
   11242              :                 ;
   11243              : 
   11244              : set_clause_list:
   11245           18 :                         set_clause                                                      { $$ = $1; }
   11246            1 :                         | set_clause_list ',' set_clause        { $$ = list_concat($1,$3); }
   11247              :                 ;
   11248              : 
   11249              : set_clause:
   11250              :                         set_target '=' a_expr
   11251              :                                 {
   11252           19 :                                         $1->val = (Node *) $3;
   11253           19 :                                         $$ = list_make1($1);
   11254              :                                 }
   11255              :                         | '(' set_target_list ')' '=' a_expr
   11256              :                                 {
   11257            0 :                                         int ncolumns = list_length($2);
   11258            0 :                                         int i = 1;
   11259              :                                         ListCell *col_cell;
   11260              : 
   11261              :                                         /* Create a MultiAssignRef source for each target */
   11262            0 :                                         foreach(col_cell, $2)
   11263              :                                         {
   11264            0 :                                                 ResTarget *res_col = (ResTarget *) lfirst(col_cell);
   11265            0 :                                                 MultiAssignRef *r = makeNode(MultiAssignRef);
   11266              : 
   11267            0 :                                                 r->source = (Node *) $5;
   11268            0 :                                                 r->colno = i;
   11269            0 :                                                 r->ncolumns = ncolumns;
   11270            0 :                                                 res_col->val = (Node *) r;
   11271            0 :                                                 i++;
   11272              :                                         }
   11273              : 
   11274            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   11275              :                                 }
   11276              :                 ;
   11277              : 
   11278              : set_target:
   11279              :                         ColId opt_indirection
   11280              :                                 {
   11281           19 :                                         $$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
   11282           19 :                                         $$->name = $1;
   11283           19 :                                         $$->indirection = check_indirection($2, yyscanner);
   11284           19 :                                         $$->val = NULL;      /* upper production sets this */
   11285           19 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   11286              :                                 }
   11287              :                 ;
   11288              : 
   11289              : set_target_list:
   11290            0 :                         set_target                                                              { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   11291            0 :                         | set_target_list ',' set_target                { $$ = lappend($1,$3); }
   11292              :                 ;
   11293              : 
   11294              : 
   11295              : /*****************************************************************************
   11296              :  *
   11297              :  *              QUERY:
   11298              :  *                              CURSOR STATEMENTS
   11299              :  *
   11300              :  *****************************************************************************/
   11301              : DeclareCursorStmt: DECLARE cursor_name cursor_options CURSOR opt_hold FOR SelectStmt
   11302              :                                 {
   11303            6 :                                         DeclareCursorStmt *n = makeNode(DeclareCursorStmt);
   11304            6 :                                         n->portalname = $2;
   11305              :                                         /* currently we always set FAST_PLAN option */
   11306            6 :                                         n->options = $3 | $5 | CURSOR_OPT_FAST_PLAN;
   11307            6 :                                         n->query = $7;
   11308            6 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11309              :                                 }
   11310              :                 ;
   11311              : 
   11312           20 : cursor_name:    name                                            { $$ = $1; }
   11313              :                 ;
   11314              : 
   11315            6 : cursor_options: /*EMPTY*/                                       { $$ = 0; }
   11316            0 :                         | cursor_options NO SCROLL              { $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_NO_SCROLL; }
   11317            0 :                         | cursor_options SCROLL                 { $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_SCROLL; }
   11318            0 :                         | cursor_options BINARY                 { $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_BINARY; }
   11319            0 :                         | cursor_options INSENSITIVE    { $$ = $1 | CURSOR_OPT_INSENSITIVE; }
   11320              :                 ;
   11321              : 
   11322            6 : opt_hold: /* EMPTY */                                           { $$ = 0; }
   11323            0 :                         | WITH HOLD                                             { $$ = CURSOR_OPT_HOLD; }
   11324            0 :                         | WITHOUT HOLD                                  { $$ = 0; }
   11325              :                 ;
   11326              : 
   11327              : /*****************************************************************************
   11328              :  *
   11329              :  *              QUERY:
   11330              :  *                              SELECT STATEMENTS
   11331              :  *
   11332              :  *****************************************************************************/
   11333              : 
   11334              : /* A complete SELECT statement looks like this.
   11335              :  *
   11336              :  * The rule returns either a single SelectStmt node or a tree of them,
   11337              :  * representing a set-operation tree.
   11338              :  *
   11339              :  * There is an ambiguity when a sub-SELECT is within an a_expr and there
   11340              :  * are excess parentheses: do the parentheses belong to the sub-SELECT or
   11341              :  * to the surrounding a_expr?  We don't really care, but bison wants to know.
   11342              :  * To resolve the ambiguity, we are careful to define the grammar so that
   11343              :  * the decision is staved off as long as possible: as long as we can keep
   11344              :  * absorbing parentheses into the sub-SELECT, we will do so, and only when
   11345              :  * it's no longer possible to do that will we decide that parens belong to
   11346              :  * the expression.      For example, in "SELECT (((SELECT 2)) + 3)" the extra
   11347              :  * parentheses are treated as part of the sub-select.  The necessity of doing
   11348              :  * it that way is shown by "SELECT (((SELECT 2)) UNION SELECT 2)".    Had we
   11349              :  * parsed "((SELECT 2))" as an a_expr, it'd be too late to go back to the
   11350              :  * SELECT viewpoint when we see the UNION.
   11351              :  *
   11352              :  * This approach is implemented by defining a nonterminal select_with_parens,
   11353              :  * which represents a SELECT with at least one outer layer of parentheses,
   11354              :  * and being careful to use select_with_parens, never '(' SelectStmt ')',
   11355              :  * in the expression grammar.  We will then have shift-reduce conflicts
   11356              :  * which we can resolve in favor of always treating '(' <select> ')' as
   11357              :  * a select_with_parens.  To resolve the conflicts, the productions that
   11358              :  * conflict with the select_with_parens productions are manually given
   11359              :  * precedences lower than the precedence of ')', thereby ensuring that we
   11360              :  * shift ')' (and then reduce to select_with_parens) rather than trying to
   11361              :  * reduce the inner <select> nonterminal to something else.  We use UMINUS
   11362              :  * precedence for this, which is a fairly arbitrary choice.
   11363              :  *
   11364              :  * To be able to define select_with_parens itself without ambiguity, we need
   11365              :  * a nonterminal select_no_parens that represents a SELECT structure with no
   11366              :  * outermost parentheses.  This is a little bit tedious, but it works.
   11367              :  *
   11368              :  * In non-expression contexts, we use SelectStmt which can represent a SELECT
   11369              :  * with or without outer parentheses.
   11370              :  */
   11371              : 
   11372              : SelectStmt: select_no_parens                    %prec UMINUS
   11373              :                         | select_with_parens            %prec UMINUS
   11374              :                 ;
   11375              : 
   11376              : select_with_parens:
   11377           91 :                         '(' select_no_parens ')'                                { $$ = $2; }
   11378            0 :                         | '(' select_with_parens ')'                    { $$ = $2; }
   11379              :                 ;
   11380              : 
   11381              : /*
   11382              :  * This rule parses the equivalent of the standard's <query expression>.
   11383              :  * The duplicative productions are annoying, but hard to get rid of without
   11384              :  * creating shift/reduce conflicts.
   11385              :  *
   11386              :  *      The locking clause (FOR UPDATE etc) may be before or after LIMIT/OFFSET.
   11387              :  *      In <=7.2.X, LIMIT/OFFSET had to be after FOR UPDATE
   11388              :  *      We now support both orderings, but prefer LIMIT/OFFSET before the locking
   11389              :  * clause.
   11390              :  *      2002-08-28 bjm
   11391              :  */
   11392              : select_no_parens:
   11393          516 :                         simple_select                                           { $$ = $1; }
   11394              :                         | select_clause sort_clause
   11395              :                                 {
   11396           15 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, NIL,
   11397              :                                                                                 NULL, NULL,
   11398              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11399           15 :                                         $$ = $1;
   11400              :                                 }
   11401              :                         | select_clause opt_sort_clause for_locking_clause opt_select_limit
   11402              :                                 {
   11403           12 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, $3,
   11404           12 :                                                                                 $4,
   11405              :                                                                                 NULL,
   11406              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11407           12 :                                         $$ = $1;
   11408              :                                 }
   11409              :                         | select_clause opt_sort_clause select_limit opt_for_locking_clause
   11410              :                                 {
   11411            3 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $1, $2, $4,
   11412            3 :                                                                                 $3,
   11413              :                                                                                 NULL,
   11414              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11415            3 :                                         $$ = $1;
   11416              :                                 }
   11417              :                         | with_clause select_clause
   11418              :                                 {
   11419            0 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, NULL, NIL,
   11420              :                                                                                 NULL,
   11421            0 :                                                                                 $1,
   11422              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11423            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   11424              :                                 }
   11425              :                         | with_clause select_clause sort_clause
   11426              :                                 {
   11427            0 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, NIL,
   11428              :                                                                                 NULL,
   11429            0 :                                                                                 $1,
   11430              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11431            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   11432              :                                 }
   11433              :                         | with_clause select_clause opt_sort_clause for_locking_clause opt_select_limit
   11434              :                                 {
   11435            0 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, $4,
   11436            0 :                                                                                 $5,
   11437            0 :                                                                                 $1,
   11438              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11439            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   11440              :                                 }
   11441              :                         | with_clause select_clause opt_sort_clause select_limit opt_for_locking_clause
   11442              :                                 {
   11443            0 :                                         insertSelectOptions((SelectStmt *) $2, $3, $5,
   11444            0 :                                                                                 $4,
   11445            0 :                                                                                 $1,
   11446              :                                                                                 yyscanner);
   11447            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   11448              :                                 }
   11449              :                 ;
   11450              : 
   11451              : select_clause:
   11452          110 :                         simple_select                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   11453            0 :                         | select_with_parens                                    { $$ = $1; }
   11454              :                 ;
   11455              : 
   11456              : /*
   11457              :  * This rule parses SELECT statements that can appear within set operations,
   11458              :  * including UNION, INTERSECT and EXCEPT.  '(' and ')' can be used to specify
   11459              :  * the ordering of the set operations.  Without '(' and ')' we want the
   11460              :  * operations to be ordered per the precedence specs at the head of this file.
   11461              :  *
   11462              :  * As with select_no_parens, simple_select cannot have outer parentheses,
   11463              :  * but can have parenthesized subclauses.
   11464              :  *
   11465              :  * Note that sort clauses cannot be included at this level --- SQL requires
   11466              :  *              SELECT foo UNION SELECT bar ORDER BY baz
   11467              :  * to be parsed as
   11468              :  *              (SELECT foo UNION SELECT bar) ORDER BY baz
   11469              :  * not
   11470              :  *              SELECT foo UNION (SELECT bar ORDER BY baz)
   11471              :  * Likewise for WITH, FOR UPDATE and LIMIT.  Therefore, those clauses are
   11472              :  * described as part of the select_no_parens production, not simple_select.
   11473              :  * This does not limit functionality, because you can reintroduce these
   11474              :  * clauses inside parentheses.
   11475              :  *
   11476              :  * NOTE: only the leftmost component SelectStmt should have INTO.
   11477              :  * However, this is not checked by the grammar; parse analysis must check it.
   11478              :  */
   11479              : simple_select:
   11480              :                         SELECT opt_all_clause opt_target_list
   11481              :                         into_clause from_clause where_clause
   11482              :                         group_clause having_clause window_clause
   11483              :                                 {
   11484          450 :                                         SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
   11485          450 :                                         n->targetList = $3;
   11486          450 :                                         n->intoClause = $4;
   11487          450 :                                         n->fromClause = $5;
   11488          450 :                                         n->whereClause = $6;
   11489          450 :                                         n->groupClause = $7;
   11490          450 :                                         n->havingClause = $8;
   11491          450 :                                         n->windowClause = $9;
   11492          450 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11493              :                                 }
   11494              :                         | SELECT distinct_clause target_list
   11495              :                         into_clause from_clause where_clause
   11496              :                         group_clause having_clause window_clause
   11497              :                                 {
   11498            4 :                                         SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
   11499            4 :                                         n->distinctClause = $2;
   11500            4 :                                         n->targetList = $3;
   11501            4 :                                         n->intoClause = $4;
   11502            4 :                                         n->fromClause = $5;
   11503            4 :                                         n->whereClause = $6;
   11504            4 :                                         n->groupClause = $7;
   11505            4 :                                         n->havingClause = $8;
   11506            4 :                                         n->windowClause = $9;
   11507            4 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11508              :                                 }
   11509          132 :                         | values_clause                                                 { $$ = $1; }
   11510              :                         | TABLE relation_expr
   11511              :                                 {
   11512              :                                         /* same as SELECT * FROM relation_expr */
   11513            0 :                                         ColumnRef *cr = makeNode(ColumnRef);
   11514            0 :                                         ResTarget *rt = makeNode(ResTarget);
   11515            0 :                                         SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
   11516              : 
   11517            0 :                                         cr->fields = list_make1(makeNode(A_Star));
   11518            0 :                                         cr->location = -1;
   11519              : 
   11520            0 :                                         rt->name = NULL;
   11521            0 :                                         rt->indirection = NIL;
   11522            0 :                                         rt->val = (Node *)cr;
   11523            0 :                                         rt->location = -1;
   11524              : 
   11525            0 :                                         n->targetList = list_make1(rt);
   11526            0 :                                         n->fromClause = list_make1($2);
   11527            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   11528              :                                 }
   11529              :                         | select_clause UNION all_or_distinct select_clause
   11530              :                                 {
   11531           40 :                                         $$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_UNION, $3, $1, $4);
   11532              :                                 }
   11533              :                         | select_clause INTERSECT all_or_distinct select_clause
   11534              :                                 {
   11535            0 :                                         $$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_INTERSECT, $3, $1, $4);
   11536              :                                 }
   11537              :                         | select_clause EXCEPT all_or_distinct select_clause
   11538              :                                 {
   11539            0 :                                         $$ = makeSetOp(SETOP_EXCEPT, $3, $1, $4);
   11540              :                                 }
   11541              :                 ;
   11542              : 
   11543              : /*
   11544              :  * SQL standard WITH clause looks like:
   11545              :  *
   11546              :  * WITH [ RECURSIVE ] <query name> [ (<column>,...) ]
   11547              :  *              AS (query) [ SEARCH or CYCLE clause ]
   11548              :  *
   11549              :  * We don't currently support the SEARCH or CYCLE clause.
   11550              :  *
   11551              :  * Recognizing WITH_LA here allows a CTE to be named TIME or ORDINALITY.
   11552              :  */
   11553              : with_clause:
   11554              :                 WITH cte_list
   11555              :                         {
   11556            1 :                                 $$ = makeNode(WithClause);
   11557            1 :                                 $$->ctes = $2;
   11558            1 :                                 $$->recursive = false;
   11559            1 :                                 $$->location = @1;
   11560              :                         }
   11561              :                 | WITH_LA cte_list
   11562              :                         {
   11563            0 :                                 $$ = makeNode(WithClause);
   11564            0 :                                 $$->ctes = $2;
   11565            0 :                                 $$->recursive = false;
   11566            0 :                                 $$->location = @1;
   11567              :                         }
   11568              :                 | WITH RECURSIVE cte_list
   11569              :                         {
   11570            0 :                                 $$ = makeNode(WithClause);
   11571            0 :                                 $$->ctes = $3;
   11572            0 :                                 $$->recursive = true;
   11573            0 :                                 $$->location = @1;
   11574              :                         }
   11575              :                 ;
   11576              : 
   11577              : cte_list:
   11578            1 :                 common_table_expr                                               { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   11579            0 :                 | cte_list ',' common_table_expr                { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
   11580              :                 ;
   11581              : 
   11582              : common_table_expr:  name opt_name_list AS opt_materialized '(' PreparableStmt ')'
   11583              :                         {
   11584            1 :                                 CommonTableExpr *n = makeNode(CommonTableExpr);
   11585            1 :                                 n->ctename = $1;
   11586            1 :                                 n->aliascolnames = $2;
   11587            1 :                                 n->ctematerialized = $4;
   11588            1 :                                 n->ctequery = $6;
   11589            1 :                                 n->location = @1;
   11590            1 :                                 $$ = (Node *) n;
   11591              :                         }
   11592              :                 ;
   11593              : 
   11594              : opt_materialized:
   11595            0 :                 MATERIALIZED                                                    { $$ = CTEMaterializeAlways; }
   11596            0 :                 | NOT MATERIALIZED                                              { $$ = CTEMaterializeNever; }
   11597            1 :                 | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = CTEMaterializeDefault; }
   11598              :                 ;
   11599              : 
   11600              : opt_with_clause:
   11601            1 :                 with_clause                                                             { $$ = $1; }
   11602          196 :                 | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NULL; }
   11603              :                 ;
   11604              : 
   11605              : into_clause:
   11606              :                         INTO OptTempTableName
   11607              :                                 {
   11608            0 :                                         $$ = makeNode(IntoClause);
   11609            0 :                                         $$->rel = $2;
   11610            0 :                                         $$->colNames = NIL;
   11611            0 :                                         $$->options = NIL;
   11612            0 :                                         $$->onCommit = ONCOMMIT_NOOP;
   11613            0 :                                         $$->tableSpaceName = NULL;
   11614            0 :                                         $$->viewQuery = NULL;
   11615            0 :                                         $$->skipData = false;
   11616              :                                 }
   11617              :                         | /*EMPTY*/
   11618          454 :                                 { $$ = NULL; }

   11767              : limit_clause:

   11776              :                         | LIMIT select_limit_value ',' select_offset_value

   11790              :                          * we can see the ONLY token in the lookahead slot.
   11791              :                          */
   11792              :                         | FETCH first_or_next select_fetch_first_value row_or_rows ONLY
   11793              :                                 {

   11834              : select_limit_value:
   11835            3 :                         a_expr                                                                  { $$ = $1; }
   11836              :                         | ALL
   11837              :                                 {
   11838              :                                         /* LIMIT ALL is represented as a NULL constant */
   11839            0 :                                         $$ = makeNullAConst(@1);
   11840              :                                 }
   11841              :                 ;
   11842              : 
   11843              : select_offset_value:
   11844            0 :                         a_expr                                                                  { $$ = $1; }
   11845              :                 ;
   11846              : 
   11847              : /*
   11848              :  * Allowing full expressions without parentheses causes various parsing
   11849              :  * problems with the trailing ROW/ROWS key words.  SQL spec only calls for
   11850              :  * <simple value specification>, which is either a literal or a parameter (but
   11851              :  * an <SQL parameter reference> could be an identifier, bringing up conflicts
   11852              :  * with ROW/ROWS). We solve this by leveraging the presence of ONLY (see above)
   11853              :  * to determine whether the expression is missing rather than trying to make it
   11854              :  * optional in this rule.
   11855              :  *
   11856              :  * c_expr covers almost all the spec-required cases (and more), but it doesn't
   11857              :  * cover signed numeric literals, which are allowed by the spec. So we include
   11858              :  * those here explicitly. We need FCONST as well as ICONST because values that
   11859              :  * don't fit in the platform's "long", but do fit in bigint, should still be
   11860              :  * accepted here. (This is possible in 64-bit Windows as well as all 32-bit
   11861              :  * builds.)
   11862              :  */
   11863              : select_fetch_first_value:
   11864            0 :                         c_expr                                                                  { $$ = $1; }
   11865              :                         | '+' I_or_F_const
   11866            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", NULL, $2, @1); }
   11867              :                         | '-' I_or_F_const
   11868            0 :                                 { $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }
   11869              :                 ;
   11870              : 
   11871              : I_or_F_const:
   11872            0 :                         Iconst                                                                  { $$ = makeIntConst($1,@1); }
   11873            0 :                         | FCONST                                                                { $$ = makeFloatConst($1,@1); }
   11874              :                 ;
   11875              : 
   11876              : /* noise words */
   11877            0 : row_or_rows: ROW                                                                        { $$ = 0; }
   11878            0 :                         | ROWS                                                                  { $$ = 0; }
   11879              :                 ;
   11880              : 
   11881            0 : first_or_next: FIRST_P                                                          { $$ = 0; }
   11882            0 :                         | NEXT                                                                  { $$ = 0; }
   11883              :                 ;
   11884              : 
   11885              : 
   11886              : /*
   11887              :  * This syntax for group_clause tries to follow the spec quite closely.
   11888              :  * However, the spec allows only column references, not expressions,
   11889              :  * which introduces an ambiguity between implicit row constructors
   11890              :  * (a,b) and lists of column references.
   11891              :  *
   11892              :  * We handle this by using the a_expr production for what the spec calls
   11893              :  * <ordinary grouping set>, which in the spec represents either one column
   11894              :  * reference or a parenthesized list of column references. Then, we check the
   11895              :  * top node of the a_expr to see if it's an implicit RowExpr, and if so, just
   11896              :  * grab and use the list, discarding the node. (this is done in parse analysis,
   11897              :  * not here)
   11898              :  *
   11899              :  * (we abuse the row_format field of RowExpr to distinguish implicit and
   11900              :  * explicit row constructors; it's debatable if anyone sanely wants to use them
   11901              :  * in a group clause, but if they have a reason to, we make it possible.)
   11902              :  *
   11903              :  * Each item in the group_clause list is either an expression tree or a
   11904              :  * GroupingSet node of some type.
   11905              :  */
   11906              : group_clause:
   11907            6 :                         GROUP_P BY group_by_list                                { $$ = $3; }
   11908          448 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NIL; }
   11909              :                 ;
   11910              : 
   11911              : group_by_list:
   11912            6 :                         group_by_item                                                   { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   11913            8 :                         | group_by_list ',' group_by_item               { $$ = lappend($1,$3); }
   11914              :                 ;
   11915              : 
   11916              : group_by_item:
   11917           14 :                         a_expr                                                                  { $$ = $1; }
   11918            0 :                         | empty_grouping_set                                    { $$ = $1; }
   11919            0 :                         | cube_clause                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   11920            0 :                         | rollup_clause                                                 { $$ = $1; }
   11921            0 :                         | grouping_sets_clause                                  { $$ = $1; }
   11922              :                 ;
   11923              : 
   11924              : empty_grouping_set:
   11925              :                         '(' ')'
   11926              :                                 {
   11927            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_EMPTY, NIL, @1);
   11928              :                                 }
   11929              :                 ;
   11930              : 
   11931              : /*
   11932              :  * These hacks rely on setting precedence of CUBE and ROLLUP below that of '(',
   11933              :  * so that they shift in these rules rather than reducing the conflicting
   11934              :  * unreserved_keyword rule.
   11935              :  */
   11936              : 
   11937              : rollup_clause:
   11938              :                         ROLLUP '(' expr_list ')'
   11939              :                                 {
   11940            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_ROLLUP, $3, @1);
   11941              :                                 }
   11942              :                 ;
   11943              : 
   11944              : cube_clause:
   11945              :                         CUBE '(' expr_list ')'
   11946              :                                 {
   11947            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_CUBE, $3, @1);
   11948              :                                 }
   11949              :                 ;
   11950              : 
   11951              : grouping_sets_clause:
   11952              :                         GROUPING SETS '(' group_by_list ')'
   11953              :                                 {
   11954            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeGroupingSet(GROUPING_SET_SETS, $4, @1);
   11955              :                                 }
   11956              :                 ;
   11957              : 
   11958              : having_clause:

   12001              : 
   12002              : /*
   12003              :  * We should allow ROW '(' expr_list ')' too, but that seems to require
   12004              :  * making VALUES a fully reserved word, which will probably break more apps
   12005              :  * than allowing the noise-word is worth.
   12006              :  */
   12007              : values_clause:
   12008              :                         VALUES '(' expr_list ')'
   12009              :                                 {
   12010          132 :                                         SelectStmt *n = makeNode(SelectStmt);
   12011          132 :                                         n->valuesLists = list_make1($3);
   12012          132 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12013              :                                 }
   12014              :                         | values_clause ',' '(' expr_list ')'
   12015              :                                 {
   12016           43 :                                         SelectStmt *n = (SelectStmt *) $1;
   12017           43 :                                         n->valuesLists = lappend(n->valuesLists, $4);
   12018           43 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12019              :                                 }
   12020              :                 ;
   12021              : 
   12022              : 
   12023              : /*****************************************************************************
   12024              :  *
   12025              :  *      clauses common to all Optimizable Stmts:
   12026              :  *              from_clause             - allow list of both JOIN expressions and table names
   12027              :  *              where_clause    - qualifications for joins or restrictions
   12028              :  *
   12029              :  *****************************************************************************/
   12030              : 
   12031              : from_clause:
   12032          376 :                         FROM from_list                                                  { $$ = $2; }
   12033           96 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NIL; }
   12034              :                 ;
   12035              : 
   12036              : from_list:
   12037          376 :                         table_ref                                                               { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   12038          127 :                         | from_list ',' table_ref                               { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
   12039              :                 ;
   12040              : 
   12041              : /*
   12042              :  * table_ref is where an alias clause can be attached.
   12043              :  */
   12044              : table_ref:      relation_expr opt_alias_clause
   12045              :                                 {
   12046          595 :                                         $1->alias = $2;
   12047          595 :                                         $$ = (Node *) $1;
   12048              :                                 }
   12049              :                         | relation_expr opt_alias_clause tablesample_clause

   12055            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12056              :                                 }
   12057              :                         | func_table func_alias_clause
   12058              :                                 {
   12059           39 :                                         RangeFunction *n = (RangeFunction *) $1;
   12060           39 :                                         n->alias = linitial($2);
   12061           39 :                                         n->coldeflist = lsecond($2);
   12062           39 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12063              :                                 }
   12064              :                         | LATERAL_P func_table func_alias_clause
   12065              :                                 {
   12066            1 :                                         RangeFunction *n = (RangeFunction *) $2;
   12067            1 :                                         n->lateral = true;
   12068            1 :                                         n->alias = linitial($3);
   12069            1 :                                         n->coldeflist = lsecond($3);
   12070            1 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12071              :                                 }
   12072              :                         | xmltable opt_alias_clause
   12073              :                                 {
   12074            0 :                                         RangeTableFunc *n = (RangeTableFunc *) $1;
   12075            0 :                                         n->alias = $2;
   12076            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12077              :                                 }
   12078              :                         | LATERAL_P xmltable opt_alias_clause
   12079              :                                 {
   12080            0 :                                         RangeTableFunc *n = (RangeTableFunc *) $2;
   12081            0 :                                         n->lateral = true;
   12082            0 :                                         n->alias = $3;
   12083            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   12084              :                                 }
   12085              :                         | select_with_parens opt_alias_clause
   12086              :                                 {
   12087           38 :                                         RangeSubselect *n = makeNode(RangeSubselect);
   12088           38 :                                         n->lateral = false;
   12089           38 :                                         n->subquery = $1;
   12090           38 :                                         n->alias = $2;
   12091              :                                         /*
   12092              :                                          * The SQL spec does not permit a subselect
   12093              :                                          * (<derived_table>) without an alias clause,
   12094              :                                          * so we don't either.  This avoids the problem
   12095              :                                          * of needing to invent a unique refname for it.
   12096              :                                          * That could be surmounted if there's sufficient
   12097              :                                          * popular demand, but for now let's just implement
   12098              :                                          * the spec and see if anyone complains.
   12099              :                                          * However, it does seem like a good idea to emit
   12100              :                                          * an error message that's better than "syntax error".
   12101              :                                          */
   12102           38 :                                         if ($2 == NULL)
   12103              :                                         {
   12104            0 :                                                 if (IsA($1, SelectStmt) &&
   12105            0 :                                                         ((SelectStmt *) $1)->valuesLists)
   12106            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   12107              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   12108              :                                                                          errmsg("VALUES in FROM must have an alias"),
   12109              :                                                                          errhint("For example, FROM (VALUES ...) [AS] foo."),
   12110              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   12111              :                                                 else
   12112            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   12113              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   12114              :                                                                          errmsg("subquery in FROM must have an alias"),

   12155              : 
   12156              : 
   12157              : /*
   12158              :  * It may seem silly to separate joined_table from table_ref, but there is
   12159              :  * method in SQL's madness: if you don't do it this way you get reduce-
   12160              :  * reduce conflicts, because it's not clear to the parser generator whether
   12161              :  * to expect alias_clause after ')' or not.  For the same reason we must
   12162              :  * treat 'JOIN' and 'join_type JOIN' separately, rather than allowing
   12163              :  * join_type to expand to empty; if we try it, the parser generator can't
   12164              :  * figure out when to reduce an empty join_type right after table_ref.
   12165              :  *
   12166              :  * Note that a CROSS JOIN is the same as an unqualified
   12167              :  * INNER JOIN, and an INNER JOIN/ON has the same shape
   12168              :  * but a qualification expression to limit membership.
   12169              :  * A NATURAL JOIN implicitly matches column names between
   12170              :  * tables and the shape is determined by which columns are
   12171              :  * in common. We'll collect columns during the later transformations.
   12172              :  */
   12173              : 
   12174              : joined_table:
   12175              :                         '(' joined_table ')'
   12176              :                                 {
   12177           22 :                                         $$ = $2;
   12178              :                                 }
   12179              :                         | table_ref CROSS JOIN table_ref
   12180              :                                 {
   12181              :                                         /* CROSS JOIN is same as unqualified inner join */
   12182            0 :                                         JoinExpr *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
   12183            0 :                                         n->jointype = JOIN_INNER;
   12184            0 :                                         n->isNatural = false;
   12185            0 :                                         n->larg = $1;
   12186            0 :                                         n->rarg = $4;
   12187            0 :                                         n->usingClause = NIL;
   12188            0 :                                         n->quals = NULL;
   12189            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   12190              :                                 }
   12191              :                         | table_ref join_type JOIN table_ref join_qual
   12192              :                                 {
   12193           82 :                                         JoinExpr *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
   12194           82 :                                         n->jointype = $2;
   12195           82 :                                         n->isNatural = false;
   12196           82 :                                         n->larg = $1;
   12197           82 :                                         n->rarg = $4;
   12198           82 :                                         if ($5 != NULL && IsA($5, List))
   12199            0 :                                                 n->usingClause = (List *) $5; /* USING clause */
   12200              :                                         else
   12201           82 :                                                 n->quals = $5; /* ON clause */
   12202           82 :                                         $$ = n;
   12203              :                                 }
   12204              :                         | table_ref JOIN table_ref join_qual
   12205              :                                 {
   12206              :                                         /* letting join_type reduce to empty doesn't work */
   12207           89 :                                         JoinExpr *n = makeNode(JoinExpr);
   12208           89 :                                         n->jointype = JOIN_INNER;
   12209           89 :                                         n->isNatural = false;
   12210           89 :                                         n->larg = $1;
   12211           89 :                                         n->rarg = $3;
   12212           89 :                                         if ($4 != NULL && IsA($4, List))
   12213            0 :                                                 n->usingClause = (List *) $4; /* USING clause */
   12214              :                                         else
   12215           89 :                                                 n->quals = $4; /* ON clause */
   12216           89 :                                         $$ = n;
   12217              :                                 }
   12218              :                         | table_ref NATURAL join_type JOIN table_ref
   12219              :                                 {

   12222            0 :                                         n->isNatural = true;

   12224            0 :                                         n->rarg = $5;
   12225            0 :                                         n->usingClause = NIL; /* figure out which columns later... */
   12226            0 :                                         n->quals = NULL; /* fill later */
   12227            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   12228              :                                 }
   12229              :                         | table_ref NATURAL JOIN table_ref

   12233            0 :                                         n->jointype = JOIN_INNER;

   12314              : /* JOIN qualification clauses
   12315              :  * Possibilities are:
   12316              :  *      USING ( column list ) allows only unqualified column names,
   12317              :  *                                                which must match between tables.
   12318              :  *      ON expr allows more general qualifications.
   12319              :  *
   12320              :  * We return USING as a List node, while an ON-expr will not be a List.
   12321              :  */

   12323            0 : join_qual:      USING '(' name_list ')'                                 { $$ = (Node *) $3; }

   12336              :                         | qualified_name '*'
   12337              :                                 {
   12338              :                                         /* inheritance query, explicitly */
   12339            0 :                                         $$ = $1;

   12365              : 
   12366              : /*
   12367              :  * Given "UPDATE foo set set ...", we have to decide without looking any
   12368              :  * further ahead whether the first "set" is an alias or the UPDATE's SET
   12369              :  * keyword.  Since "set" is allowed as a column name both interpretations
   12370              :  * are feasible.  We resolve the shift/reduce conflict by giving the first
   12371              :  * relation_expr_opt_alias production a higher precedence than the SET token
   12372              :  * has, causing the parser to prefer to reduce, in effect assuming that the
   12373              :  * SET is not an alias.
   12374              :  */

   12385              :                                 }

   12416              : /*
   12417              :  * func_table represents a function invocation in a FROM list. It can be
   12418              :  * a plain function call, like "foo(...)", or a ROWS FROM expression with
   12419              :  * one or more function calls, "ROWS FROM (foo(...), bar(...))",
   12420              :  * optionally with WITH ORDINALITY attached.
   12421              :  * In the ROWS FROM syntax, a column definition list can be given for each
   12422              :  * function, for example:
   12423              :  *     ROWS FROM (foo() AS (foo_res_a text, foo_res_b text),
   12424              :  *                bar() AS (bar_res_a text, bar_res_b text))
   12425              :  * It's also possible to attach a column definition list to the RangeFunction
   12426              :  * as a whole, but that's handled by the table_ref production.
   12427              :  */

   12612              :                                                                                  parser_errposition(defel->location)));
   12613            0 :                                                         fc->is_not_null = intVal(defel->arg);
   12614            0 :                                                         nullability_seen = true;
   12615              :                                                 }
   12616              :                                                 else
   12617              :                                                 {
   12618            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   12619              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   12620              :                                                                          errmsg("unrecognized column option \"%s\"",
   12621              :                                                                                         defel->defname),
   12622              :                                                                          parser_errposition(defel->location)));
   12623              :                                                 }
   12624              :                                         }
   12625            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) fc;
   12626              :                                 }
   12627              :                         | ColId FOR ORDINALITY
   12628              :                                 {
   12629            0 :                                         RangeTableFuncCol          *fc = makeNode(RangeTableFuncCol);
   12630              : 
   12631            0 :                                         fc->colname = $1;
   12632            0 :                                         fc->for_ordinality = true;
   12633              :                                         /* other fields are ignored, initialized by makeNode */
   12634            0 :                                         fc->location = @1;
   12635              : 
   12636            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) fc;
   12637              :                                 }
   12638              :                 ;

   12642            0 :                                 { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   12643              :                         | xmltable_column_option_list xmltable_column_option_el

   12645              :                 ;

   12647              : xmltable_column_option_el:

   12665              : xml_namespace_el:
   12666              :                         b_expr AS ColLabel

   12670            0 :                                         $$->indirection = NIL;
   12671            0 :                                         $$->val = $1;
   12672            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12673              :                                 }
   12674              :                         | DEFAULT b_expr
   12675              :                                 {

   12677            0 :                                         $$->name = NULL;
