   12681              :                                 }
   12682              :                 ;
   12683              : 
   12684              : /*****************************************************************************
   12685              :  *
   12686              :  *      Type syntax
   12687              :  *              SQL introduces a large amount of type-specific syntax.
   12688              :  *              Define individual clauses to handle these cases, and use
   12689              :  *               the generic case to handle regular type-extensible Postgres syntax.
   12690              :  *              - thomas 1997-10-10
   12691              :  *
   12692              :  *****************************************************************************/
   12693              : 
   12694              : Typename:       SimpleTypename opt_array_bounds
   12695              :                                 {
   12696         1289 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12697         1289 :                                         $$->arrayBounds = $2;
   12698              :                                 }
   12699              :                         | SETOF SimpleTypename opt_array_bounds
   12700              :                                 {
   12701           11 :                                         $$ = $2;
   12702           11 :                                         $$->arrayBounds = $3;
   12703           11 :                                         $$->setof = true;
   12704              :                                 }
   12705              :                         /* SQL standard syntax, currently only one-dimensional */
   12706              :                         | SimpleTypename ARRAY '[' Iconst ']'
   12707              :                                 {
   12708            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12709            0 :                                         $$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger($4));
   12710              :                                 }
   12711              :                         | SETOF SimpleTypename ARRAY '[' Iconst ']'
   12712              :                                 {
   12713            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   12714            0 :                                         $$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger($5));
   12715            0 :                                         $$->setof = true;
   12716              :                                 }
   12717              :                         | SimpleTypename ARRAY
   12718              :                                 {
   12719            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12720            0 :                                         $$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger(-1));
   12721              :                                 }
   12722              :                         | SETOF SimpleTypename ARRAY
   12723              :                                 {
   12724            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   12725            0 :                                         $$->arrayBounds = list_make1(makeInteger(-1));
   12726            0 :                                         $$->setof = true;
   12727              :                                 }
   12728              :                 ;
   12729              : 
   12730              : opt_array_bounds:
   12731              :                         opt_array_bounds '[' ']'
   12732           15 :                                         {  $$ = lappend($1, makeInteger(-1)); }
   12733              :                         | opt_array_bounds '[' Iconst ']'
   12734            0 :                                         {  $$ = lappend($1, makeInteger($3)); }
   12735              :                         | /*EMPTY*/
   12736         1300 :                                         {  $$ = NIL; }
   12737              :                 ;
   12738              : 
   12739              : SimpleTypename:
   12740         1048 :                         GenericType                                                             { $$ = $1; }
   12741          248 :                         | Numeric                                                               { $$ = $1; }
   12742            0 :                         | Bit                                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   12743            2 :                         | Character                                                             { $$ = $1; }
   12744            1 :                         | ConstDatetime                                                 { $$ = $1; }
   12745              :                         | ConstInterval opt_interval
   12746              :                                 {
   12747            1 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12748            1 :                                         $$->typmods = $2;
   12749              :                                 }
   12750              :                         | ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')'
   12751              :                                 {
   12752            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12753            0 :                                         $$->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),
   12754              :                                                                                          makeIntConst($3, @3));
   12755              :                                 }
   12756              :                 ;
   12757              : 
   12758              : /* We have a separate ConstTypename to allow defaulting fixed-length
   12759              :  * types such as CHAR() and BIT() to an unspecified length.
   12760              :  * SQL9x requires that these default to a length of one, but this
   12761              :  * makes no sense for constructs like CHAR 'hi' and BIT '0101',
   12762              :  * where there is an obvious better choice to make.
   12763              :  * Note that ConstInterval is not included here since it must
   12764              :  * be pushed up higher in the rules to accommodate the postfix
   12765              :  * options (e.g. INTERVAL '1' YEAR). Likewise, we have to handle
   12766              :  * the generic-type-name case in AexprConst to avoid premature
   12767              :  * reduce/reduce conflicts against function names.
   12768              :  */
   12769              : ConstTypename:
   12770            0 :                         Numeric                                                                 { $$ = $1; }
   12771            0 :                         | ConstBit                                                              { $$ = $1; }
   12772            0 :                         | ConstCharacter                                                { $$ = $1; }
   12773            0 :                         | ConstDatetime                                                 { $$ = $1; }
   12774              :                 ;
   12775              : 
   12776              : /*
   12777              :  * GenericType covers all type names that don't have special syntax mandated
   12778              :  * by the standard, including qualified names.  We also allow type modifiers.
   12779              :  * To avoid parsing conflicts against function invocations, the modifiers
   12780              :  * have to be shown as expr_list here, but parse analysis will only accept
   12781              :  * constants for them.
   12782              :  */
   12783              : GenericType:
   12784              :                         type_function_name opt_type_modifiers
   12785              :                                 {
   12786         1041 :                                         $$ = makeTypeName($1);
   12787         1041 :                                         $$->typmods = $2;
   12788         1041 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12789              :                                 }
   12790              :                         | type_function_name attrs opt_type_modifiers
   12791              :                                 {
   12792            7 :                                         $$ = makeTypeNameFromNameList(lcons(makeString($1), $2));
   12793            7 :                                         $$->typmods = $3;
   12794            7 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12795              :                                 }
   12796              :                 ;
   12797              : 
   12798            0 : opt_type_modifiers: '(' expr_list ')'                           { $$ = $2; }
   12799         1048 :                                         | /* EMPTY */                                   { $$ = NIL; }
   12800              :                 ;
   12801              : 
   12802              : /*
   12803              :  * SQL numeric data types
   12804              :  */
   12805              : Numeric:        INT_P
   12806              :                                 {
   12807          158 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("int4");
   12808          158 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12809              :                                 }
   12810              :                         | INTEGER
   12811              :                                 {
   12812           10 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("int4");
   12813           10 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12814              :                                 }
   12815              :                         | SMALLINT
   12816              :                                 {
   12817            1 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("int2");
   12818            1 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12819              :                                 }
   12820              :                         | BIGINT
   12821              :                                 {
   12822           38 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("int8");
   12823           38 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12824              :                                 }
   12825              :                         | REAL
   12826              :                                 {
   12827            0 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("float4");
   12828            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12829              :                                 }
   12830              :                         | FLOAT_P opt_float
   12831              :                                 {
   12832            0 :                                         $$ = $2;
   12833            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12834              :                                 }
   12835              :                         | DOUBLE_P PRECISION
   12836              :                                 {
   12837            1 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("float8");
   12838            1 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12839              :                                 }
   12840              :                         | DECIMAL_P opt_type_modifiers
   12841              :                                 {
   12842            0 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("numeric");
   12843            0 :                                         $$->typmods = $2;
   12844            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12845              :                                 }
   12846              :                         | DEC opt_type_modifiers
   12847              :                                 {
   12848            0 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("numeric");
   12849            0 :                                         $$->typmods = $2;
   12850            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12851              :                                 }
   12852              :                         | NUMERIC opt_type_modifiers
   12853              :                                 {
   12854            0 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("numeric");
   12855            0 :                                         $$->typmods = $2;
   12856            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12857              :                                 }
   12858              :                         | BOOLEAN_P
   12859              :                                 {

   12861           40 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12862              :                                 }
   12863              :                 ;
   12864              : 
   12865              : opt_float:      '(' Iconst ')'
   12866              :                                 {
   12867              :                                         /*
   12868              :                                          * Check FLOAT() precision limits assuming IEEE floating
   12869              :                                          * types - thomas 1997-09-18
   12870              :                                          */
   12871            0 :                                         if ($2 < 1)
   12872            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   12873              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   12874              :                                                                  errmsg("precision for type float must be at least 1 bit"),
   12875              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@2)));
   12876            0 :                                         else if ($2 <= 24)
   12877            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("float4");
   12878            0 :                                         else if ($2 <= 53)
   12879            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("float8");
   12880              :                                         else
   12881            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   12882              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
   12883              :                                                                  errmsg("precision for type float must be less than 54 bits"),
   12884              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@2)));
   12885              :                                 }
   12886              :                         | /*EMPTY*/
   12887              :                                 {
   12888            0 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName("float8");
   12889              :                                 }
   12890              :                 ;
   12891              : 
   12892              : /*
   12893              :  * SQL bit-field data types
   12894              :  * The following implements BIT() and BIT VARYING().
   12895              :  */
   12896              : Bit:            BitWithLength
   12897              :                                 {
   12898            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12899              :                                 }
   12900              :                         | BitWithoutLength
   12901              :                                 {
   12902            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12903              :                                 }
   12904              :                 ;
   12905              : 
   12906              : /* ConstBit is like Bit except "BIT" defaults to unspecified length */
   12907              : /* See notes for ConstCharacter, which addresses same issue for "CHAR" */
   12908              : ConstBit:       BitWithLength
   12909              :                                 {
   12910            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12911              :                                 }
   12912              :                         | BitWithoutLength
   12913              :                                 {
   12914            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12915            0 :                                         $$->typmods = NIL;
   12916              :                                 }
   12917              :                 ;
   12918              : 
   12919              : BitWithLength:
   12920              :                         BIT opt_varying '(' expr_list ')'
   12921              :                                 {
   12922              :                                         char *typname;
   12923              : 
   12924            0 :                                         typname = $2 ? "varbit" : "bit";
   12925            0 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName(typname);
   12926            0 :                                         $$->typmods = $4;
   12927            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12928              :                                 }
   12929              :                 ;
   12930              : 
   12931              : BitWithoutLength:
   12932              :                         BIT opt_varying
   12933              :                                 {
   12934              :                                         /* bit defaults to bit(1), varbit to no limit */
   12935            0 :                                         if ($2)
   12936              :                                         {
   12937            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("varbit");
   12938              :                                         }
   12939              :                                         else
   12940              :                                         {
   12941            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("bit");
   12942            0 :                                                 $$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst(1, -1));
   12943              :                                         }
   12944            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12945              :                                 }
   12946              :                 ;
   12947              : 
   12948              : 
   12949              : /*
   12950              :  * SQL character data types
   12951              :  * The following implements CHAR() and VARCHAR().
   12952              :  */
   12953              : Character:  CharacterWithLength
   12954              :                                 {
   12955            1 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12956              :                                 }
   12957              :                         | CharacterWithoutLength
   12958              :                                 {
   12959            1 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12960              :                                 }
   12961              :                 ;
   12962              : 
   12963              : ConstCharacter:  CharacterWithLength
   12964              :                                 {
   12965            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12966              :                                 }
   12967              :                         | CharacterWithoutLength
   12968              :                                 {
   12969              :                                         /* Length was not specified so allow to be unrestricted.
   12970              :                                          * This handles problems with fixed-length (bpchar) strings
   12971              :                                          * which in column definitions must default to a length
   12972              :                                          * of one, but should not be constrained if the length
   12973              :                                          * was not specified.
   12974              :                                          */
   12975            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   12976            0 :                                         $$->typmods = NIL;
   12977              :                                 }
   12978              :                 ;
   12979              : 
   12980              : CharacterWithLength:  character '(' Iconst ')'
   12981              :                                 {
   12982            1 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName($1);
   12983            1 :                                         $$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));
   12984            1 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12985              :                                 }
   12986              :                 ;
   12987              : 
   12988              : CharacterWithoutLength:  character
   12989              :                                 {
   12990            1 :                                         $$ = SystemTypeName($1);
   12991              :                                         /* char defaults to char(1), varchar to no limit */
   12992            1 :                                         if (strcmp($1, "bpchar") == 0)
   12993            0 :                                                 $$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst(1, -1));
   12994            1 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   12995              :                                 }
   12996              :                 ;
   12997              : 
   12998              : character:      CHARACTER opt_varying
   12999            2 :                                                                                 { $$ = $2 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
   13000              :                         | CHAR_P opt_varying
   13001            0 :                                                                                 { $$ = $2 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
   13002              :                         | VARCHAR
   13003            0 :                                                                                 { $$ = "varchar"; }
   13004              :                         | NATIONAL CHARACTER opt_varying
   13005            0 :                                                                                 { $$ = $3 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
   13006              :                         | NATIONAL CHAR_P opt_varying
   13007            0 :                                                                                 { $$ = $3 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
   13008              :                         | NCHAR opt_varying
   13009            0 :                                                                                 { $$ = $2 ? "varchar": "bpchar"; }
   13010              :                 ;
   13011              : 
   13012              : opt_varying:
   13013            2 :                         VARYING                                                                 { $$ = true; }
   13014            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = false; }
   13015              :                 ;

   13017              : /*
   13018              :  * SQL date/time types

   13020              : ConstDatetime:
   13021              :                         TIMESTAMP '(' Iconst ')' opt_timezone
   13022              :                                 {
   13023            1 :                                         if ($5)
   13024            1 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("timestamptz");
   13025              :                                         else
   13026            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("timestamp");
   13027            1 :                                         $$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));
   13028            1 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   13029              :                                 }
   13030              :                         | TIMESTAMP opt_timezone
   13031              :                                 {
   13032            0 :                                         if ($2)
   13033            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("timestamptz");
   13034              :                                         else
   13035            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("timestamp");
   13036            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   13037              :                                 }
   13038              :                         | TIME '(' Iconst ')' opt_timezone
   13039              :                                 {
   13040            0 :                                         if ($5)
   13041            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("timetz");
   13042              :                                         else
   13043            0 :                                                 $$ = SystemTypeName("time");
   13044            0 :                                         $$->typmods = list_make1(makeIntConst($3, @3));
   13045            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   13046              :                                 }
   13047              :                         | TIME opt_timezone

   13049            0 :                                         if ($2)

   13057              : ConstInterval:

   13064              : 
   13065              : opt_timezone:
   13066            1 :                         WITH_LA TIME ZONE                                               { $$ = true; }
   13067            0 :                         | WITHOUT TIME ZONE                                             { $$ = false; }
   13068            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = false; }
   13069              :                 ;

   13071              : opt_interval:
   13072              :                         YEAR_P
   13073            0 :                                 { $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(YEAR), @1)); }
   13074              :                         | MONTH_P
   13075            0 :                                 { $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MONTH), @1)); }
   13076              :                         | DAY_P
   13077            0 :                                 { $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY), @1)); }
   13078              :                         | HOUR_P
   13079            0 :                                 { $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR), @1)); }
   13080              :                         | MINUTE_P
   13081            0 :                                 { $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1)); }
   13082              :                         | interval_second
   13083            0 :                                 { $$ = $1; }
   13084              :                         | YEAR_P TO MONTH_P
   13085              :                                 {
   13086            0 :                                         $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(YEAR) |
   13087              :                                                                                                  INTERVAL_MASK(MONTH), @1));
   13088              :                                 }
   13089              :                         | DAY_P TO HOUR_P
   13090              :                                 {

   13093              :                                 }
   13094              :                         | DAY_P TO MINUTE_P
   13095              :                                 {
   13096            0 :                                         $$ = list_make1(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_MASK(DAY) |

   13098              :                                                                                                  INTERVAL_MASK(MINUTE), @1));
   13099              :                                 }
   13100              :                         | DAY_P TO interval_second
   13101              :                                 {

   13104              :                                                                                                 INTERVAL_MASK(HOUR) |

   13107              :                                 }
   13108              :                         | HOUR_P TO MINUTE_P
   13109              :                                 {

   13112              :                                 }

   13129              : 
   13130              : interval_second:

   13139              :                                 }
   13140              :                 ;
   13141              : 
   13142              : 
   13143              : /*****************************************************************************
   13144              :  *
   13145              :  *      expression grammar
   13146              :  *
   13147              :  *****************************************************************************/
   13148              : 
   13149              : /*
   13150              :  * General expressions
   13151              :  * This is the heart of the expression syntax.
   13152              :  *
   13153              :  * We have two expression types: a_expr is the unrestricted kind, and
   13154              :  * b_expr is a subset that must be used in some places to avoid shift/reduce
   13155              :  * conflicts.  For example, we can't do BETWEEN as "BETWEEN a_expr AND a_expr"
   13156              :  * because that use of AND conflicts with AND as a boolean operator.  So,
   13157              :  * b_expr is used in BETWEEN and we remove boolean keywords from b_expr.
   13158              :  *
   13159              :  * Note that '(' a_expr ')' is a b_expr, so an unrestricted expression can
   13160              :  * always be used by surrounding it with parens.
   13161              :  *
   13162              :  * c_expr is all the productions that are common to a_expr and b_expr;
   13163              :  * it's factored out just to eliminate redundant coding.
   13164              :  *
   13165              :  * Be careful of productions involving more than one terminal token.
   13166              :  * By default, bison will assign such productions the precedence of their
   13167              :  * last terminal, but in nearly all cases you want it to be the precedence
   13168              :  * of the first terminal instead; otherwise you will not get the behavior
   13169              :  * you expect!  So we use %prec annotations freely to set precedences.
   13170              :  */
   13171         6395 : a_expr:         c_expr                                                                  { $$ = $1; }
   13172              :                         | a_expr TYPECAST Typename
   13173          131 :                                         { $$ = makeTypeCast($1, $3, @2); }
   13174              :                         | a_expr COLLATE any_name
   13175              :                                 {
   13176            0 :                                         CollateClause *n = makeNode(CollateClause);
   13177            0 :                                         n->arg = $1;
   13178            0 :                                         n->collname = $3;
   13179            0 :                                         n->location = @2;
   13180            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   13181              :                                 }
   13182              :                         | a_expr AT TIME ZONE a_expr                    %prec AT
   13183              :                                 {
   13184            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("timezone"),
   13185            0 :                                                                                            list_make2($5, $1),
   13186            0 :                                                                                            @2);
   13187              :                                 }
   13188              :                 /*
   13189              :                  * These operators must be called out explicitly in order to make use
   13190              :                  * of bison's automatic operator-precedence handling.  All other
   13191              :                  * operator names are handled by the generic productions using "Op",
   13192              :                  * below; and all those operators will have the same precedence.
   13193              :                  *
   13194              :                  * If you add more explicitly-known operators, be sure to add them
   13195              :                  * also to b_expr and to the MathOp list below.
   13196              :                  */
   13197              :                         | '+' a_expr                                    %prec UMINUS
   13198            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", NULL, $2, @1); }
   13199              :                         | '-' a_expr                                    %prec UMINUS
   13200           17 :                                 { $$ = doNegate($2, @1); }
   13201              :                         | a_expr '+' a_expr
   13202           11 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "+", $1, $3, @2); }
   13203              :                         | a_expr '-' a_expr
   13204           12 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "-", $1, $3, @2); }
   13205              :                         | a_expr '*' a_expr
   13206            1 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "*", $1, $3, @2); }
   13207              :                         | a_expr '/' a_expr
   13208            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "/", $1, $3, @2); }
   13209              :                         | a_expr '%' a_expr
   13210            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "%", $1, $3, @2); }
   13211              :                         | a_expr '^' a_expr
   13212            1 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "^", $1, $3, @2); }
   13213              :                         | a_expr '<' a_expr
   13214            8 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<", $1, $3, @2); }
   13215              :                         | a_expr '>' a_expr
   13216           14 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, ">", $1, $3, @2); }
   13217              :                         | a_expr '=' a_expr
   13218          569 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "=", $1, $3, @2); }
   13219              :                         | a_expr LESS_EQUALS a_expr
   13220            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<=", $1, $3, @2); }
   13221              :                         | a_expr GREATER_EQUALS a_expr
   13222            6 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, ">=", $1, $3, @2); }
   13223              :                         | a_expr NOT_EQUALS a_expr
   13224           27 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "<>", $1, $3, @2); }
   13225              : 
   13226              :                         | a_expr qual_Op a_expr                         %prec Op
   13227           87 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, $3, @2); }
   13228              :                         | qual_Op a_expr                                        %prec Op
   13229            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $1, NULL, $2, @1); }
   13230              :                         | a_expr qual_Op                                        %prec POSTFIXOP
   13231            0 :                                 { $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, $2, $1, NULL, @2); }
   13232              : 
   13233              :                         | a_expr AND a_expr
   13234          359 :                                 { $$ = makeAndExpr($1, $3, @2); }
   13235              :                         | a_expr OR a_expr
   13236           78 :                                 { $$ = makeOrExpr($1, $3, @2); }
   13237              :                         | NOT a_expr
   13238           33 :                                 { $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }
   13239              :                         | NOT_LA a_expr                                         %prec NOT
   13240            0 :                                 { $$ = makeNotExpr($2, @1); }
   13241              : 
   13242              :                         | a_expr LIKE a_expr
   13243              :                                 {
   13244            2 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, "~~",
   13245            2 :                                                                                                    $1, $3, @2);
   13246              :                                 }
   13247              :                         | a_expr LIKE a_expr ESCAPE a_expr                                      %prec LIKE
   13248              :                                 {
   13249            0 :                                         FuncCall *n = makeFuncCall(SystemFuncName("like_escape"),
   13250            0 :                                                                                            list_make2($3, $5),
   13251            0 :                                                                                            @2);

   13253            0 :                                                                                                    $1, (Node *) n, @2);
   13254              :                                 }
   13255              :                         | a_expr NOT_LA LIKE a_expr                                                     %prec NOT_LA
   13256              :                                 {

   13264            0 :                                                                                            @2);
   13265            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_LIKE, "!~~",
   13266            0 :                                                                                                    $1, (Node *) n, @2);
   13267              :                                 }
   13268              :                         | a_expr ILIKE a_expr
   13269              :                                 {

   13271            0 :                                                                                                    $1, $3, @2);
   13272              :                                 }

   13327              : 
   13328              :                         /* NullTest clause
   13329              :                          * Define SQL-style Null test clause.
   13330              :                          * Allow two forms described in the standard:
   13331              :                          *      a IS NULL
   13332              :                          *      a IS NOT NULL
   13333              :                          * Allow two SQL extensions
   13334              :                          *      a ISNULL
   13335              :                          *      a NOTNULL
   13336              :                          */
   13337              :                         | a_expr IS NULL_P                                                      %prec IS
   13338              :                                 {
   13339           24 :                                         NullTest *n = makeNode(NullTest);
   13340           24 :                                         n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
   13341           24 :                                         n->nulltesttype = IS_NULL;
   13342           24 :                                         n->location = @2;
   13343           24 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   13344              :                                 }
   13345              :                         | a_expr ISNULL
   13346              :                                 {
   13347            0 :                                         NullTest *n = makeNode(NullTest);
   13348            0 :                                         n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
   13349            0 :                                         n->nulltesttype = IS_NULL;
   13350            0 :                                         n->location = @2;
   13351            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   13352              :                                 }
   13353              :                         | a_expr IS NOT NULL_P                                          %prec IS
   13354              :                                 {
   13355           51 :                                         NullTest *n = makeNode(NullTest);
   13356           51 :                                         n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
   13357           51 :                                         n->nulltesttype = IS_NOT_NULL;
   13358           51 :                                         n->location = @2;
   13359           51 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   13360              :                                 }
   13361              :                         | a_expr NOTNULL
   13362              :                                 {
   13363            0 :                                         NullTest *n = makeNode(NullTest);
   13364            0 :                                         n->arg = (Expr *) $1;
   13365            0 :                                         n->nulltesttype = IS_NOT_NULL;
   13366            0 :                                         n->location = @2;
   13367            0 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   13368              :                                 }

   13481              :                         | a_expr IN_P in_expr
   13482              :                                 {
   13483              :                                         /* in_expr returns a SubLink or a list of a_exprs */
   13484           82 :                                         if (IsA($3, SubLink))
   13485              :                                         {
   13486              :                                                 /* generate foo = ANY (subquery) */
   13487           12 :                                                 SubLink *n = (SubLink *) $3;
   13488           12 :                                                 n->subLinkType = ANY_SUBLINK;
   13489           12 :                                                 n->subLinkId = 0;
   13490           12 :                                                 n->testexpr = $1;
   13491           12 :                                                 n->operName = NIL;           /* show it's IN not = ANY */
   13492           12 :                                                 n->location = @2;
   13493           12 :                                                 $$ = (Node *)n;
   13494              :                                         }
   13495              :                                         else
   13496              :                                         {
   13497              :                                                 /* generate scalar IN expression */
   13498           70 :                                                 $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_IN, "=", $1, $3, @2);
   13499              :                                         }
   13500              :                                 }
   13501              :                         | a_expr NOT_LA IN_P in_expr                                            %prec NOT_LA
   13502              :                                 {

   13504            7 :                                         if (IsA($4, SubLink))
   13505              :                                         {
   13506              :                                                 /* generate NOT (foo = ANY (subquery)) */
   13507              :                                                 /* Make an = ANY node */
   13508            0 :                                                 SubLink *n = (SubLink *) $4;
   13509            0 :                                                 n->subLinkType = ANY_SUBLINK;
   13510            0 :                                                 n->subLinkId = 0;
   13511            0 :                                                 n->testexpr = $1;
   13512            0 :                                                 n->operName = NIL;           /* show it's IN not = ANY */
   13513            0 :                                                 n->location = @2;
   13514              :                                                 /* Stick a NOT on top; must have same parse location */
   13515            0 :                                                 $$ = makeNotExpr((Node *) n, @2);
   13516              :                                         }
   13517              :                                         else
   13518              :                                         {
   13519              :                                                 /* generate scalar NOT IN expression */
   13520            7 :                                                 $$ = (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_IN, "<>", $1, $4, @2);
   13521              :                                         }
   13522              :                                 }
   13523              :                         | a_expr subquery_Op sub_type select_with_parens        %prec Op
   13524              :                                 {
   13525            1 :                                         SubLink *n = makeNode(SubLink);
   13526            1 :                                         n->subLinkType = $3;
   13527            1 :                                         n->subLinkId = 0;
   13528            1 :                                         n->testexpr = $1;
   13529            1 :                                         n->operName = $2;
   13530            1 :                                         n->subselect = $4;
   13531            1 :                                         n->location = @2;
   13532            1 :                                         $$ = (Node *)n;
   13533              :                                 }

   13535              :                                 {
   13536            4 :                                         if ($3 == ANY_SUBLINK)
   13537            4 :                                                 $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP_ANY, $2, $1, $5, @2);
   13538              :                                         else
   13539            0 :                                                 $$ = (Node *) makeA_Expr(AEXPR_OP_ALL, $2, $1, $5, @2);
   13540              :                                 }
   13541              :                         | UNIQUE select_with_parens
   13542              :                                 {
   13543              :                                         /* Not sure how to get rid of the parentheses
   13544              :                                          * but there are lots of shift/reduce errors without them.
   13545              :                                          *
   13546              :                                          * Should be able to implement this by plopping the entire
   13547              :                                          * select into a node, then transforming the target expressions
   13548              :                                          * from whatever they are into count(*), and testing the
   13549              :                                          * entire result equal to one.
   13550              :                                          * But, will probably implement a separate node in the executor.
   13551              :                                          */
   13552            0 :                                         ereport(ERROR,
   13553              :                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
   13554              :                                                          errmsg("UNIQUE predicate is not yet implemented"),
   13555              :                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   13556              :                                 }

   13558              :                                 {

   13716              :                                                  * warnings from being issued when the ordering has

   13718              :                                                  * AEXPR_PAREN node has the same exprLocation as its

   13890              : 
   13891              : 
   13892              : /*
   13893              :  * func_expr and its cousin func_expr_windowless are split out from c_expr just
   13894              :  * so that we have classifications for "everything that is a function call or
   13895              :  * looks like one".  This isn't very important, but it saves us having to
   13896              :  * document which variants are legal in places like "FROM function()" or the
   13897              :  * backwards-compatible functional-index syntax for CREATE INDEX.
   13898              :  * (Note that many of the special SQL functions wouldn't actually make any
   13899              :  * sense as functional index entries, but we ignore that consideration here.)
   13900              :  */
   13901              : func_expr: func_application within_group_clause filter_clause over_clause
   13902              :                                 {
   13903          542 :                                         FuncCall *n = (FuncCall *) $1;
   13904              :                                         /*
   13905              :                                          * The order clause for WITHIN GROUP and the one for
   13906              :                                          * plain-aggregate ORDER BY share a field, so we have to
   13907              :                                          * check here that at most one is present.  We also check
   13908              :                                          * for DISTINCT and VARIADIC here to give a better error
   13909              :                                          * location.  Other consistency checks are deferred to
   13910              :                                          * parse analysis.
   13911              :                                          */
   13912          542 :                                         if ($2 != NIL)
   13913              :                                         {
   13914            0 :                                                 if (n->agg_order != NIL)
   13915            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   13916              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   13917              :                                                                          errmsg("cannot use multiple ORDER BY clauses with WITHIN GROUP"),
   13918              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@2)));
   13919            0 :                                                 if (n->agg_distinct)
   13920            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   13921              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   13922              :                                                                          errmsg("cannot use DISTINCT with WITHIN GROUP"),
   13923              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@2)));
   13924            0 :                                                 if (n->func_variadic)
   13925            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   13926              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   13927              :                                                                          errmsg("cannot use VARIADIC with WITHIN GROUP"),
   13928              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@2)));
   13929            0 :                                                 n->agg_order = $2;
   13930            0 :                                                 n->agg_within_group = true;
   13931              :                                         }
   13932          542 :                                         n->agg_filter = $3;
   13933          542 :                                         n->over = $4;
   13934          542 :                                         $$ = (Node *) n;
   13935              :                                 }

   13945              :  */
   13946              : func_expr_windowless:

   14331              : 
   14332              : /*
   14333              :  * If we see PARTITION, RANGE, ROWS or GROUPS as the first token after the '('
   14334              :  * of a window_specification, we want the assumption to be that there is
   14335              :  * no existing_window_name; but those keywords are unreserved and so could
   14336              :  * be ColIds.  We fix this by making them have the same precedence as IDENT
   14337              :  * and giving the empty production here a slightly higher precedence, so
   14338              :  * that the shift/reduce conflict is resolved in favor of reducing the rule.
   14339              :  * These keywords are thus precluded from being an existing_window_name but
   14340              :  * are not reserved for any other purpose.
   14341              :  */
   14342            0 : opt_existing_window_name: ColId                                         { $$ = $1; }
   14343            1 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                             %prec Op                { $$ = NULL; }
   14344              :                 ;
   14345              : 
   14346            1 : opt_partition_clause: PARTITION BY expr_list            { $$ = $3; }
   14347            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NIL; }
   14348              :                 ;
   14349              : 
   14350              : /*
   14351              :  * For frame clauses, we return a WindowDef, but only some fields are used:
   14352              :  * frameOptions, startOffset, and endOffset.
   14353              :  */
   14354              : opt_frame_clause:
   14355              :                         RANGE frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause
   14356              :                                 {
   14357            0 :                                         WindowDef *n = $2;
   14358            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_RANGE;
   14359            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= $3;
   14360            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   14361              :                                 }
   14362              :                         | ROWS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause
   14363              :                                 {
   14364            0 :                                         WindowDef *n = $2;
   14365            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_ROWS;
   14366            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= $3;
   14367            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   14368              :                                 }
   14369              :                         | GROUPS frame_extent opt_window_exclusion_clause
   14370              :                                 {
   14371            0 :                                         WindowDef *n = $2;
   14372            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_NONDEFAULT | FRAMEOPTION_GROUPS;
   14373            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= $3;
   14374            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   14375              :                                 }
   14376              :                         | /*EMPTY*/
   14377              :                                 {
   14378            1 :                                         WindowDef *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
   14379            1 :                                         n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_DEFAULTS;
   14380            1 :                                         n->startOffset = NULL;
   14381            1 :                                         n->endOffset = NULL;
   14382            1 :                                         $$ = n;
   14383              :                                 }
   14384              :                 ;
   14385              : 
   14386              : frame_extent: frame_bound
   14387              :                                 {
   14388            0 :                                         WindowDef *n = $1;
   14389              :                                         /* reject invalid cases */
   14390            0 :                                         if (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)
   14391            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   14392              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
   14393              :                                                                  errmsg("frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING"),
   14394              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@1)));
   14395            0 :                                         if (n->frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING)
   14396            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   14397              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
   14398              :                                                                  errmsg("frame starting from following row cannot end with current row"),
   14399              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@1)));
   14400            0 :                                         n->frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW;
   14401            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   14402              :                                 }
   14403              :                         | BETWEEN frame_bound AND frame_bound
   14404              :                                 {
   14405            0 :                                         WindowDef *n1 = $2;
   14406            0 :                                         WindowDef *n2 = $4;
   14407              :                                         /* form merged options */
   14408            0 :                                         int             frameOptions = n1->frameOptions;
   14409              :                                         /* shift converts START_ options to END_ options */
   14410            0 :                                         frameOptions |= n2->frameOptions << 1;
   14411            0 :                                         frameOptions |= FRAMEOPTION_BETWEEN;
   14412              :                                         /* reject invalid cases */
   14413            0 :                                         if (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING)
   14414            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   14415              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
   14416              :                                                                  errmsg("frame start cannot be UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING"),
   14417              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@2)));
   14418            0 :                                         if (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING)
   14419            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   14420              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
   14421              :                                                                  errmsg("frame end cannot be UNBOUNDED PRECEDING"),
   14422              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@4)));
   14423            0 :                                         if ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_CURRENT_ROW) &&
   14424            0 :                                                 (frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING))
   14425            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   14426              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
   14427              :                                                                  errmsg("frame starting from current row cannot have preceding rows"),
   14428              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@4)));
   14429            0 :                                         if ((frameOptions & FRAMEOPTION_START_OFFSET_FOLLOWING) &&
   14430            0 :                                                 (frameOptions & (FRAMEOPTION_END_OFFSET_PRECEDING |
   14431              :                                                                                  FRAMEOPTION_END_CURRENT_ROW)))
   14432            0 :                                                 ereport(ERROR,
   14433              :                                                                 (errcode(ERRCODE_WINDOWING_ERROR),
   14434              :                                                                  errmsg("frame starting from following row cannot have preceding rows"),
   14435              :                                                                  parser_errposition(@4)));
   14436            0 :                                         n1->frameOptions = frameOptions;
   14437            0 :                                         n1->endOffset = n2->startOffset;
   14438            0 :                                         $$ = n1;
   14439              :                                 }
   14440              :                 ;
   14441              : 
   14442              : /*
   14443              :  * This is used for both frame start and frame end, with output set up on
   14444              :  * the assumption it's frame start; the frame_extent productions must reject
   14445              :  * invalid cases.
   14446              :  */
   14447              : frame_bound:
   14448              :                         UNBOUNDED PRECEDING
   14449              :                                 {
   14450            0 :                                         WindowDef *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
   14451            0 :                                         n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_PRECEDING;
   14452            0 :                                         n->startOffset = NULL;
   14453            0 :                                         n->endOffset = NULL;
   14454            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   14455              :                                 }
   14456              :                         | UNBOUNDED FOLLOWING
   14457              :                                 {
   14458            0 :                                         WindowDef *n = makeNode(WindowDef);
   14459            0 :                                         n->frameOptions = FRAMEOPTION_START_UNBOUNDED_FOLLOWING;
   14460            0 :                                         n->startOffset = NULL;
   14461            0 :                                         n->endOffset = NULL;
   14462            0 :                                         $$ = n;
   14463              :                                 }

   14490              : opt_window_exclusion_clause:
   14491            0 :                         EXCLUDE CURRENT_P ROW   { $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_CURRENT_ROW; }
   14492            0 :                         | EXCLUDE GROUP_P               { $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_GROUP; }
   14493            0 :                         | EXCLUDE TIES                  { $$ = FRAMEOPTION_EXCLUDE_TIES; }

   14495            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                             { $$ = 0; }

   14497              : 
   14498              : 
   14499              : /*
   14500              :  * Supporting nonterminals for expressions.
   14501              :  */
   14502              : 
   14503              : /* Explicit row production.
   14504              :  *
   14505              :  * SQL99 allows an optional ROW keyword, so we can now do single-element rows
   14506              :  * without conflicting with the parenthesized a_expr production.  Without the
   14507              :  * ROW keyword, there must be more than one a_expr inside the parens.
   14508              :  */
   14509            0 : row:            ROW '(' expr_list ')'                                   { $$ = $3; }
   14510            0 :                         | ROW '(' ')'                                                   { $$ = NIL; }
   14511            0 :                         | '(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'                  { $$ = lappend($2, $4); }
   14512              :                 ;
   14513              : 
   14514            2 : explicit_row:   ROW '(' expr_list ')'                           { $$ = $3; }
   14515            0 :                         | ROW '(' ')'                                                   { $$ = NIL; }
   14516              :                 ;
   14517              : 
   14518           11 : implicit_row:   '(' expr_list ',' a_expr ')'            { $$ = lappend($2, $4); }
   14519              :                 ;
   14520              : 
   14521            5 : sub_type:       ANY                                                                             { $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }
   14522            0 :                         | SOME                                                                  { $$ = ANY_SUBLINK; }
   14523            0 :                         | ALL                                                                   { $$ = ALL_SUBLINK; }
   14524              :                 ;
   14525              : 
   14526            2 : all_Op:         Op                                                                              { $$ = $1; }
   14527           33 :                         | MathOp                                                                { $$ = $1; }
   14528              :                 ;
   14529              : 
   14530            0 : MathOp:          '+'                                                                    { $$ = "+"; }
   14531            0 :                         | '-'                                                                   { $$ = "-"; }
   14532            0 :                         | '*'                                                                   { $$ = "*"; }
   14533            0 :                         | '/'                                                                   { $$ = "/"; }
   14534            0 :                         | '%'                                                                   { $$ = "%"; }
   14535            0 :                         | '^'                                                                   { $$ = "^"; }
   14536            0 :                         | '<'                                                                        { $$ = "<"; }
   14537            0 :                         | '>'                                                                        { $$ = ">"; }
   14538           32 :                         | '='                                                                   { $$ = "="; }
   14539            0 :                         | LESS_EQUALS                                                   { $$ = "<="; }
   14540            0 :                         | GREATER_EQUALS                                                { $$ = ">="; }
   14541            1 :                         | NOT_EQUALS                                                    { $$ = "<>"; }
   14542              :                 ;

   14570              : /* cannot put SIMILAR TO here, because SIMILAR TO is a hack.
   14571              :  * the regular expression is preprocessed by a function (similar_to_escape),
   14572              :  * and the ~ operator for posix regular expressions is used.
   14573              :  *        x SIMILAR TO y     ->    x ~ similar_to_escape(y)
   14574              :  * this transformation is made on the fly by the parser upwards.
   14575              :  * however the SubLink structure which handles any/some/all stuff
   14576              :  * is not ready for such a thing.
   14577              :  */
   14578              :                         ;

   14656              : /* Allow delimited string Sconst in extract_arg as an SQL extension.

   14658              :  */
   14659              : extract_arg:
   14660            0 :                         IDENT                                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   14661            0 :                         | YEAR_P                                                                { $$ = "year"; }
   14662            0 :                         | MONTH_P                                                               { $$ = "month"; }
   14663            0 :                         | DAY_P                                                                 { $$ = "day"; }
   14664            0 :                         | HOUR_P                                                                { $$ = "hour"; }

   14676              : 
   14677              : /* OVERLAY() arguments
   14678              :  * SQL99 defines the OVERLAY() function:
   14679              :  * o overlay(text placing text from int for int)
   14680              :  * o overlay(text placing text from int)
   14681              :  * and similarly for binary strings
   14682              :  */
   14683              : overlay_list:
   14684              :                         a_expr overlay_placing substr_from substr_for
   14685              :                                 {
   14686            0 :                                         $$ = list_make4($1, $2, $3, $4);
   14687              :                                 }
   14688              :                         | a_expr overlay_placing substr_from
   14689              :                                 {
   14690            0 :                                         $$ = list_make3($1, $2, $3);
   14691              :                                 }
   14692              :                 ;
   14693              : 
   14694              : overlay_placing:
   14695              :                         PLACING a_expr
   14696            0 :                                 { $$ = $2; }
   14697              :                 ;
   14698              : 
   14699              : /* position_list uses b_expr not a_expr to avoid conflict with general IN */
   14700              : 
   14701              : position_list:
   14702            1 :                         b_expr IN_P b_expr                                              { $$ = list_make2($3, $1); }
   14703            0 :                         | /*EMPTY*/                                                             { $$ = NIL; }
   14704              :                 ;
   14705              : 
   14706              : /* SUBSTRING() arguments
   14707              :  * SQL9x defines a specific syntax for arguments to SUBSTRING():
   14708              :  * o substring(text from int for int)
   14709              :  * o substring(text from int) get entire string from starting point "int"
   14710              :  * o substring(text for int) get first "int" characters of string
   14711              :  * o substring(text from pattern) get entire string matching pattern
   14712              :  * o substring(text from pattern for escape) same with specified escape char
   14713              :  * We also want to support generic substring functions which accept
   14714              :  * the usual generic list of arguments. So we will accept both styles
   14715              :  * here, and convert the SQL9x style to the generic list for further
   14716              :  * processing. - thomas 2000-11-28
   14717              :  */
   14718              : substr_list:
   14719              :                         a_expr substr_from substr_for
   14720              :                                 {
   14721            1 :                                         $$ = list_make3($1, $2, $3);
   14722              :                                 }
   14723              :                         | a_expr substr_for substr_from
   14724              :                                 {
   14725              :                                         /* not legal per SQL99, but might as well allow it */
   14726            0 :                                         $$ = list_make3($1, $3, $2);
   14727              :                                 }
   14728              :                         | a_expr substr_from
   14729              :                                 {
   14730            4 :                                         $$ = list_make2($1, $2);
   14731              :                                 }
   14732              :                         | a_expr substr_for
   14733              :                                 {
   14734              :                                         /*
   14735              :                                          * Since there are no cases where this syntax allows
   14736              :                                          * a textual FOR value, we forcibly cast the argument
   14737              :                                          * to int4.  The possible matches in pg_proc are
   14738              :                                          * substring(text,int4) and substring(text,text),
   14739              :                                          * and we don't want the parser to choose the latter,
   14740              :                                          * which it is likely to do if the second argument
   14741              :                                          * is unknown or doesn't have an implicit cast to int4.
   14742              :                                          */
   14743            0 :                                         $$ = list_make3($1, makeIntConst(1, -1),
   14744              :                                                                         makeTypeCast($2,
   14745              :                                                                                                  SystemTypeName("int4"), -1));
   14746              :                                 }
   14747              :                         | expr_list
   14748              :                                 {
   14749            0 :                                         $$ = $1;
   14750              :                                 }
   14751              :                         | /*EMPTY*/
   14752            0 :                                 { $$ = NIL; }
   14753              :                 ;

   14879              : /*****************************************************************************

   14884              : 
   14885          450 : opt_target_list: target_list                                            { $$ = $1; }
