   14893              : 
   14894              : target_el:      a_expr AS ColLabel
   14895              :                                 {
   14896          973 :                                         $$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
   14897          973 :                                         $$->name = $3;
   14898          973 :                                         $$->indirection = NIL;
   14899          973 :                                         $$->val = (Node *)$1;
   14900          973 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   14901              :                                 }
   14902              :                         /*
   14903              :                          * We support omitting AS only for column labels that aren't
   14904              :                          * any known keyword.  There is an ambiguity against postfix
   14905              :                          * operators: is "a ! b" an infix expression, or a postfix
   14906              :                          * expression and a column label?  We prefer to resolve this
   14907              :                          * as an infix expression, which we accomplish by assigning
   14908              :                          * IDENT a precedence higher than POSTFIXOP.
   14909              :                          */
   14910              :                         | a_expr IDENT
   14911              :                                 {
   14912            0 :                                         $$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
   14913            0 :                                         $$->name = $2;
   14914            0 :                                         $$->indirection = NIL;
   14915            0 :                                         $$->val = (Node *)$1;
   14916            0 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   14917              :                                 }
   14918              :                         | a_expr
   14919              :                                 {
   14920          846 :                                         $$ = makeNode(ResTarget);
   14921          846 :                                         $$->name = NULL;
   14922          846 :                                         $$->indirection = NIL;
   14923          846 :                                         $$->val = (Node *)$1;
   14924          846 :                                         $$->location = @1;
   14925              :                                 }
   14926              :                         | '*'

   14931              :

   14936           76 :                                         $$->location = @1;

   14940              : 
   14941              : /*****************************************************************************
   14942              :  *
   14943              :  *      Names and constants
   14944              :  *
   14945              :  *****************************************************************************/
   14946              : 
   14947              : qualified_name_list:
   14948           80 :                         qualified_name                                                  { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   14949            0 :                         | qualified_name_list ',' qualified_name { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
   14950              :                 ;
   14951              : 
   14952              : /*
   14953              :  * The production for a qualified relation name has to exactly match the
   14954              :  * production for a qualified func_name, because in a FROM clause we cannot
   14955              :  * tell which we are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' for a
   14956              :  * func_name, something else for a relation). Therefore we allow 'indirection'
   14957              :  * which may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.
   14958              :  */
   14959              : qualified_name:
   14960              :                         ColId
   14961              :                                 {
   14962         1242 :                                         $$ = makeRangeVar(NULL, $1, @1);
   14963              :                                 }
   14964              :                         | ColId indirection
   14965              :                                 {
   14966           72 :                                         check_qualified_name($2, yyscanner);
   14967           72 :                                         $$ = makeRangeVar(NULL, NULL, @1);
   14968           72 :                                         switch (list_length($2))
   14969              :                                         {
   14970           72 :                                                 case 1:
   14971           72 :                                                         $$->catalogname = NULL;
   14972           72 :                                                         $$->schemaname = $1;
   14973           72 :                                                         $$->relname = strVal(linitial($2));
   14974           72 :                                                         break;
   14975            0 :                                                 case 2:
   14976            0 :                                                         $$->catalogname = $1;
   14977            0 :                                                         $$->schemaname = strVal(linitial($2));
   14978            0 :                                                         $$->relname = strVal(lsecond($2));
   14979            0 :                                                         break;
   14980            0 :                                                 default:
   14981            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   14982              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   14983              :                                                                          errmsg("improper qualified name (too many dotted names): %s",
   14984              :                                                                                         NameListToString(lcons(makeString($1), $2))),
   14985              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   14986              :                                                         break;
   14987              :                                         }
   14988              :                                 }
   14989              :                 ;
   14990              : 
   14991              : name_list:      name
   14992           97 :                                         { $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
   14993              :                         | name_list ',' name
   14994          436 :                                         { $$ = lappend($1, makeString($3)); }
   14995              :                 ;
   14996              : 
   14997              : 
   14998          588 : name:           ColId                                                                   { $$ = $1; };
   14999              : 
   15000              : database_name:
   15001            3 :                         ColId                                                                   { $$ = $1; };
   15002              : 
   15003              : access_method:
   15004           13 :                         ColId                                                                   { $$ = $1; };
   15005              : 
   15006         2135 : attr_name:      ColLabel                                                                { $$ = $1; };
   15007              : 
   15008           19 : index_name: ColId                                                                       { $$ = $1; };
   15009              : 
   15010            0 : file_name:      Sconst                                                                  { $$ = $1; };
   15011              : 
   15012              : /*
   15013              :  * The production for a qualified func_name has to exactly match the
   15014              :  * production for a qualified columnref, because we cannot tell which we
   15015              :  * are parsing until we see what comes after it ('(' or Sconst for a func_name,
   15016              :  * anything else for a columnref).  Therefore we allow 'indirection' which
   15017              :  * may contain subscripts, and reject that case in the C code.  (If we
   15018              :  * ever implement SQL99-like methods, such syntax may actually become legal!)
   15019              :  */
   15020              : func_name:      type_function_name
   15021          657 :                                         { $$ = list_make1(makeString($1)); }
   15022              :                         | ColId indirection
   15023              :                                         {
   15024           23 :                                                 $$ = check_func_name(lcons(makeString($1), $2),
   15025              :                                                                                          yyscanner);
   15026              :                                         }
   15027              :                 ;
   15028              : 
   15029              : 
   15030              : /*
   15031              :  * Constants
   15032              :  */
   15033              : AexprConst: Iconst
   15034              :                                 {
   15035          707 :                                         $$ = makeIntConst($1, @1);
   15036              :                                 }
   15037              :                         | FCONST
   15038              :                                 {
   15039            9 :                                         $$ = makeFloatConst($1, @1);
   15040              :                                 }
   15041              :                         | Sconst
   15042              :                                 {
   15043          987 :                                         $$ = makeStringConst($1, @1);
   15044              :                                 }
   15045              :                         | BCONST
   15046              :                                 {
   15047            0 :                                         $$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);
   15048              :                                 }
   15049              :                         | XCONST
   15050              :                                 {
   15051              :                                         /* This is a bit constant per SQL99:
   15052              :                                          * Without Feature F511, "BIT data type",
   15053              :                                          * a <general literal> shall not be a
   15054              :                                          * <bit string literal> or a <hex string literal>.
   15055              :                                          */
   15056            0 :                                         $$ = makeBitStringConst($1, @1);
   15057              :                                 }
   15058              :                         | func_name Sconst
   15059              :                                 {
   15060              :                                         /* generic type 'literal' syntax */
   15061            0 :                                         TypeName *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);
   15062            0 :                                         t->location = @1;
   15063            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);
   15064              :                                 }
   15065              :                         | func_name '(' func_arg_list opt_sort_clause ')' Sconst
   15066              :                                 {
   15067              :                                         /* generic syntax with a type modifier */
   15068            0 :                                         TypeName *t = makeTypeNameFromNameList($1);
   15069              :                                         ListCell *lc;
   15070              : 
   15071              :                                         /*
   15072              :                                          * We must use func_arg_list and opt_sort_clause in the
   15073              :                                          * production to avoid reduce/reduce conflicts, but we
   15074              :                                          * don't actually wish to allow NamedArgExpr in this
   15075              :                                          * context, nor ORDER BY.
   15076              :                                          */
   15077            0 :                                         foreach(lc, $3)
   15078              :                                         {
   15079            0 :                                                 NamedArgExpr *arg = (NamedArgExpr *) lfirst(lc);
   15080              : 
   15081            0 :                                                 if (IsA(arg, NamedArgExpr))
   15082            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   15083              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   15084              :                                                                          errmsg("type modifier cannot have parameter name"),
   15085              :                                                                          parser_errposition(arg->location)));
   15086              :                                         }
   15087            0 :                                         if ($4 != NIL)
   15088            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   15089              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   15090              :                                                                          errmsg("type modifier cannot have ORDER BY"),
   15091              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@4)));
   15092              : 
   15093            0 :                                         t->typmods = $3;
   15094            0 :                                         t->location = @1;
   15095            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($6, @6, t);
   15096              :                                 }
   15097              :                         | ConstTypename Sconst
   15098              :                                 {
   15099            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, $1);
   15100              :                                 }
   15101              :                         | ConstInterval Sconst opt_interval
   15102              :                                 {
   15103            0 :                                         TypeName *t = $1;
   15104            0 :                                         t->typmods = $3;
   15105            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($2, @2, t);
   15106              :                                 }
   15107              :                         | ConstInterval '(' Iconst ')' Sconst
   15108              :                                 {
   15109            0 :                                         TypeName *t = $1;
   15110            0 :                                         t->typmods = list_make2(makeIntConst(INTERVAL_FULL_RANGE, -1),
   15111              :                                                                                         makeIntConst($3, @3));
   15112            0 :                                         $$ = makeStringConstCast($5, @5, t);
   15113              :                                 }
   15114              :                         | TRUE_P
   15115              :                                 {
   15116           17 :                                         $$ = makeBoolAConst(true, @1);
   15117              :                                 }
   15118              :                         | FALSE_P
   15119              :                                 {
   15120           25 :                                         $$ = makeBoolAConst(false, @1);
   15121              :                                 }
   15122              :                         | NULL_P
   15123              :                                 {
   15124          266 :                                         $$ = makeNullAConst(@1);
   15125              :                                 }
   15126              :                 ;
   15127              : 
   15128          729 : Iconst:         ICONST                                                                  { $$ = $1; };
   15129         1092 : Sconst:         SCONST                                                                  { $$ = $1; };
   15130              : 
   15131           15 : SignedIconst: Iconst                                                            { $$ = $1; }
   15132            0 :                         | '+' Iconst                                                    { $$ = + $2; }
   15133            0 :                         | '-' Iconst                                                    { $$ = - $2; }
   15134              :                 ;
   15135              : 
   15136              : /* Role specifications */
   15137              : RoleId:         RoleSpec
   15138              :                                 {
   15139            0 :                                         RoleSpec *spc = (RoleSpec *) $1;
   15140            0 :                                         switch (spc->roletype)
   15141              :                                         {
   15142            0 :                                                 case ROLESPEC_CSTRING:
   15143            0 :                                                         $$ = spc->rolename;
   15144            0 :                                                         break;
   15145            0 :                                                 case ROLESPEC_PUBLIC:
   15146            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   15147              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
   15148              :                                                                          errmsg("role name \"%s\" is reserved",
   15149              :                                                                                         "public"),
   15150              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   15151              :                                                         break;
   15152            0 :                                                 case ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER:
   15153            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   15154              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
   15155              :                                                                          errmsg("%s cannot be used as a role name here",
   15156              :                                                                                         "SESSION_USER"),
   15157              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   15158              :                                                         break;
   15159            0 :                                                 case ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER:
   15160            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   15161              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
   15162              :                                                                          errmsg("%s cannot be used as a role name here",
   15163              :                                                                                         "CURRENT_USER"),
   15164              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   15165              :                                                         break;
   15166              :                                         }
   15167              :                                 }
   15168              :                         ;
   15169              : 
   15170              : RoleSpec:       NonReservedWord
   15171              :                                         {
   15172              :                                                 /*
   15173              :                                                  * "public" and "none" are not keywords, but they must
   15174              :                                                  * be treated specially here.
   15175              :                                                  */
   15176              :                                                 RoleSpec *n;
   15177          125 :                                                 if (strcmp($1, "public") == 0)
   15178              :                                                 {
   15179          116 :                                                         n = (RoleSpec *) makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_PUBLIC, @1);
   15180          116 :                                                         n->roletype = ROLESPEC_PUBLIC;
   15181              :                                                 }
   15182            9 :                                                 else if (strcmp($1, "none") == 0)
   15183              :                                                 {
   15184            0 :                                                         ereport(ERROR,
   15185              :                                                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_RESERVED_NAME),
   15186              :                                                                          errmsg("role name \"%s\" is reserved",
   15187              :                                                                                         "none"),
   15188              :                                                                          parser_errposition(@1)));
   15189              :                                                 }
   15190              :                                                 else
   15191              :                                                 {
   15192            9 :                                                         n = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CSTRING, @1);
   15193            9 :                                                         n->rolename = pstrdup($1);
   15194              :                                                 }
   15195          125 :                                                 $$ = n;
   15196              :                                         }
   15197              :                         | CURRENT_USER
   15198              :                                         {
   15199            0 :                                                 $$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_CURRENT_USER, @1);
   15200              :                                         }
   15201              :                         | SESSION_USER
   15202              :                                         {
   15203            0 :                                                 $$ = makeRoleSpec(ROLESPEC_SESSION_USER, @1);
   15204              :                                         }
   15205              :                 ;
   15206              : 
   15207              : role_list:      RoleSpec
   15208            3 :                                         { $$ = list_make1($1); }
   15209              :                         | role_list ',' RoleSpec
   15210            0 :                                         { $$ = lappend($1, $3); }
   15211              :                 ;
   15212              : 
   15213              : /*
   15214              :  * Name classification hierarchy.
   15215              :  *
   15216              :  * IDENT is the lexeme returned by the lexer for identifiers that match
   15217              :  * no known keyword.  In most cases, we can accept certain keywords as
   15218              :  * names, not only IDENTs.      We prefer to accept as many such keywords
   15219              :  * as possible to minimize the impact of "reserved words" on programmers.
   15220              :  * So, we divide names into several possible classes.  The classification
   15221              :  * is chosen in part to make keywords acceptable as names wherever possible.
   15222              :  */
   15223              : 
   15224              : /* Column identifier --- names that can be column, table, etc names.
   15225              :  */
   15226         6072 : ColId:          IDENT                                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   15227          155 :                         | unreserved_keyword                                    { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15228           45 :                         | col_name_keyword                                              { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15229              :                 ;
   15230              : 
   15231              : /* Type/function identifier --- names that can be type or function names.
   15232              :  */
   15233         1701 : type_function_name:     IDENT                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   15234          151 :                         | unreserved_keyword                                    { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15235            0 :                         | type_func_name_keyword                                { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15236              :                 ;
   15237              : 
   15238              : /* Any not-fully-reserved word --- these names can be, eg, role names.
   15239              :  */
   15240          175 : NonReservedWord:        IDENT                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   15241           30 :                         | unreserved_keyword                                    { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15242            0 :                         | col_name_keyword                                              { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15243            0 :                         | type_func_name_keyword                                { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15244              :                 ;
   15245              : 
   15246              : /* Column label --- allowed labels in "AS" clauses.
   15247              :  * This presently includes *all* Postgres keywords.
   15248              :  */
   15249         3090 : ColLabel:       IDENT                                                                   { $$ = $1; }
   15250          109 :                         | unreserved_keyword                                    { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15251            0 :                         | col_name_keyword                                              { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15252            0 :                         | type_func_name_keyword                                { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15253            0 :                         | reserved_keyword                                              { $$ = pstrdup($1); }
   15254              :                 ;
   15255              : 
   15256              : 
   15257              : /*
   15258              :  * Keyword category lists.  Generally, every keyword present in
   15259              :  * the Postgres grammar should appear in exactly one of these lists.
   15260              :  *
   15261              :  * Put a new keyword into the first list that it can go into without causing
   15262              :  * shift or reduce conflicts.  The earlier lists define "less reserved"
   15263              :  * categories of keywords.
   15264              :  *
   15265              :  * Make sure that each keyword's category in kwlist.h matches where
   15266              :  * it is listed here.  (Someday we may be able to generate these lists and
   15267              :  * kwlist.h's table from a common master list.)
   15268              :  */
   15269              : 
   15270              : /* "Unreserved" keywords --- available for use as any kind of name.
   15271              :  */
   15272              : unreserved_keyword:
   15273              :                           ABORT_P
   15274              :                         | ABSOLUTE_P
   15275              :                         | ACCESS
   15276              :                         | ACTION
   15277              :                         | ADD_P
   15278              :                         | ADMIN
   15279              :                         | AFTER
   15280              :                         | AGGREGATE
   15281              :                         | ALSO
   15282              :                         | ALTER
   15283              :                         | ALWAYS
   15284              :                         | ASSERTION
   15285              :                         | ASSIGNMENT
   15286              :                         | AT
   15287              :                         | ATTACH
   15288              :                         | ATTRIBUTE
   15289              :                         | BACKWARD
   15290              :                         | BEFORE
   15291              :                         | BEGIN_P
   15292              :                         | BY
   15293              :                         | CACHE
   15294              :                         | CALL
   15295              :                         | CALLED
   15296              :                         | CASCADE
   15297              :                         | CASCADED
   15298              :                         | CATALOG_P
   15299              :                         | CHAIN
   15300              :                         | CHARACTERISTICS
   15301              :                         | CHECKPOINT
   15302              :                         | CLASS

   15304              :                         | CLUSTER

   15570              :                         | YES_P
   15571              :                         | ZONE
   15572              :                 ;
   15573              : 
   15574              : /* Column identifier --- keywords that can be column, table, etc names.
   15575              :  *
   15576              :  * Many of these keywords will in fact be recognized as type or function
   15577              :  * names too; but they have special productions for the purpose, and so
   15578              :  * can't be treated as "generic" type or function names.
   15579              :  *
   15580              :  * The type names appearing here are not usable as function names
   15581              :  * because they can be followed by '(' in typename productions, which
   15582              :  * looks too much like a function call for an LR(1) parser.
   15583              :  */
   15584              : col_name_keyword:
   15585              :                           BETWEEN
   15586              :                         | BIGINT
   15587              :                         | BIT
   15588              :                         | BOOLEAN_P
   15589              :                         | CHAR_P
   15590              :                         | CHARACTER
   15591              :                         | COALESCE
   15592              :                         | DEC
   15593              :                         | DECIMAL_P
   15594              :                         | EXISTS
   15595              :                         | EXTRACT
   15596              :                         | FLOAT_P
   15597              :                         | GREATEST
   15598              :                         | GROUPING
   15599              :                         | INOUT
   15600              :                         | INT_P
   15601              :                         | INTEGER
   15602              :                         | INTERVAL
   15603              :                         | LEAST
   15604              :                         | NATIONAL
   15605              :                         | NCHAR
   15606              :                         | NONE
   15607              :                         | NORMALIZE
   15608              :                         | NULLIF
   15609              :                         | NUMERIC
   15610              :                         | OUT_P
   15611              :                         | OVERLAY
   15612              :                         | POSITION
   15613              :                         | PRECISION
   15614              :                         | REAL
   15615              :                         | ROW
   15616              :                         | SETOF
   15617              :                         | SMALLINT
   15618              :                         | SUBSTRING
   15619              :                         | TIME
   15620              :                         | TIMESTAMP
   15621              :                         | TREAT
   15622              :                         | TRIM
   15623              :                         | VALUES
   15624              :                         | VARCHAR
   15625              :                         | XMLATTRIBUTES
   15626              :                         | XMLCONCAT
   15627              :                         | XMLELEMENT
   15628              :                         | XMLEXISTS
   15629              :                         | XMLFOREST
   15630              :                         | XMLNAMESPACES
   15631              :                         | XMLPARSE
   15632              :                         | XMLPI
   15633              :                         | XMLROOT
   15634              :                         | XMLSERIALIZE
   15635              :                         | XMLTABLE
   15636              :                 ;
   15637              : 
   15638              : /* Type/function identifier --- keywords that can be type or function names.
   15639              :  *
   15640              :  * Most of these are keywords that are used as operators in expressions;
   15641              :  * in general such keywords can't be column names because they would be
   15642              :  * ambiguous with variables, but they are unambiguous as function identifiers.
   15643              :  *
   15644              :  * Do not include POSITION, SUBSTRING, etc here since they have explicit
   15645              :  * productions in a_expr to support the goofy SQL9x argument syntax.
   15646              :  * - thomas 2000-11-28
   15647              :  */
   15648              : type_func_name_keyword:
   15649              :                           AUTHORIZATION
   15650              :                         | BINARY
   15651              :                         | COLLATION
   15652              :                         | CONCURRENTLY
   15653              :                         | CROSS
   15654              :                         | CURRENT_SCHEMA
   15655              :                         | FREEZE
   15656              :                         | FULL
   15657              :                         | ILIKE
   15658              :                         | INNER_P
   15659              :                         | IS
   15660              :                         | ISNULL
   15661              :                         | JOIN
   15662              :                         | LEFT
   15663              :                         | LIKE
   15664              :                         | NATURAL
   15665              :                         | NOTNULL
   15666              :                         | OUTER_P
   15667              :                         | OVERLAPS
   15668              :                         | RIGHT
   15669              :                         | SIMILAR
   15670              :                         | TABLESAMPLE
   15671              :                         | VERBOSE
   15672              :                 ;
   15673              : 
   15674              : /* Reserved keyword --- these keywords are usable only as a ColLabel.
   15675              :  *
   15676              :  * Keywords appear here if they could not be distinguished from variable,
   15677              :  * type, or function names in some contexts.  Don't put things here unless
   15678              :  * forced to.
   15679              :  */
   15680              : reserved_keyword:
   15681              :                           ALL
   15682              :                         | ANALYSE
   15683              :                         | ANALYZE
   15684              :                         | AND
   15685              :                         | ANY
   15686              :                         | ARRAY
   15687              :                         | AS
   15688              :                         | ASC
   15689              :                         | ASYMMETRIC
   15690              :                         | BOTH
   15691              :                         | CASE
   15692              :                         | CAST
   15693              :                         | CHECK
   15694              :                         | COLLATE
   15695              :                         | COLUMN
   15696              :                         | CONSTRAINT
   15697              :                         | CREATE
   15698              :                         | CURRENT_CATALOG
   15699              :                         | CURRENT_DATE

   15701              :                         | CURRENT_TIME

   15762              : /*

   15773              : static RawStmt *
   15774         1452 : makeRawStmt(Node *stmt, int stmt_location)
   15775              : {

   15778         1452 :         rs->stmt = stmt;
   15779         1452 :         rs->stmt_location = stmt_location;
   15780         1452 :         rs->stmt_len = 0;                    /* might get changed later */
   15781         1452 :         return rs;
   15782              : }
   15783              : 
   15784              : /* Adjust a RawStmt to reflect that it doesn't run to the end of the string */
   15785              : static void
   15786         1400 : updateRawStmtEnd(RawStmt *rs, int end_location)
   15787              : {
   15788              :         /*
   15789              :          * If we already set the length, don't change it.  This is for situations
   15790              :          * like "select foo ;; select bar" where the same statement will be last
   15791              :          * in the string for more than one semicolon.
   15792              :          */
   15793         1400 :         if (rs->stmt_len > 0)
   15794            0 :                 return;
   15795              : 
   15796              :         /* OK, update length of RawStmt */
   15797         1400 :         rs->stmt_len = end_location - rs->stmt_location;
   15798              : }

   15800              : static Node *
   15801         2736 : makeColumnRef(char *colname, List *indirection,
   15802              :                           int location, core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
   15803              : {
   15804              :         /*
   15805              :          * Generate a ColumnRef node, with an A_Indirection node added if there
   15806              :          * is any subscripting in the specified indirection list.  However,
   15807              :          * any field selection at the start of the indirection list must be
   15808              :          * transposed into the "fields" part of the ColumnRef node.
   15809              :          */
   15810         2736 :         ColumnRef  *c = makeNode(ColumnRef);
   15811         2736 :         int             nfields = 0;
   15812              :         ListCell *l;
   15813              : 
   15814         2736 :         c->location = location;
   15815         4746 :         foreach(l, indirection)
   15816              :         {
   15817         2022 :                 if (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Indices))
   15818              :                 {
   15819           12 :                         A_Indirection *i = makeNode(A_Indirection);
   15820              : 
   15821           12 :                         if (nfields == 0)
   15822              :                         {
   15823              :                                 /* easy case - all indirection goes to A_Indirection */
   15824           11 :                                 c->fields = list_make1(makeString(colname));
   15825           11 :                                 i->indirection = check_indirection(indirection, yyscanner);
   15826              :                         }
   15827              :                         else
   15828              :                         {
   15829              :                                 /* got to split the list in two */
   15830            1 :                                 i->indirection = check_indirection(list_copy_tail(indirection,
   15831              :                                                                                                                                   nfields),
   15832              :                                                                                                    yyscanner);
   15833            1 :                                 indirection = list_truncate(indirection, nfields);
   15834            1 :                                 c->fields = lcons(makeString(colname), indirection);
   15835              :                         }
   15836           12 :                         i->arg = (Node *) c;
   15837           12 :                         return (Node *) i;
   15838              :                 }
   15839         2010 :                 else if (IsA(lfirst(l), A_Star))
   15840              :                 {
   15841              :                         /* We only allow '*' at the end of a ColumnRef */
   15842           12 :                         if (lnext(indirection, l) != NULL)
   15843            0 :                                 parser_yyerror("improper use of \"*\"");
   15844              :                 }
   15845         2010 :                 nfields++;
   15846              :         }
   15847              :         /* No subscripting, so all indirection gets added to field list */
   15848         2724 :         c->fields = lcons(makeString(colname), indirection);
   15849         2724 :         return (Node *) c;
   15850              : }

   15953              : /* makeBoolAConst()

   16038              : /* extractArgTypes()

   16044              : static List *

   16052           50 :                 FunctionParameter *p = (FunctionParameter *) lfirst(i);
   16053              : 
   16054           50 :                 if (p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_OUT && p->mode != FUNC_PARAM_TABLE)
   16055           50 :                         result = lappend(result, p->argType);
   16056              :         }
   16057           50 :         return result;
   16058              : }

   16110              : /* insertSelectOptions()
   16111              :  * Insert ORDER BY, etc into an already-constructed SelectStmt.
   16112              :  *
   16113              :  * This routine is just to avoid duplicating code in SelectStmt productions.

   16115              : static void
   16116           30 : insertSelectOptions(SelectStmt *stmt,
   16117              :                                         List *sortClause, List *lockingClause,
   16118              :                                         SelectLimit *limitClause,
   16119              :                                         WithClause *withClause,
   16120              :                                         core_yyscan_t yyscanner)
   16121              : {
   16122              :         Assert(IsA(stmt, SelectStmt));
   16123              : 
   16124              :         /*
   16125              :          * Tests here are to reject constructs like
   16126              :          *      (SELECT foo ORDER BY bar) ORDER BY baz
   16127              :          */
   16128           30 :         if (sortClause)
   16129              :         {
   16130           18 :                 if (stmt->sortClause)
   16131            0 :                         ereport(ERROR,
   16132              :                                         (errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
   16133              :                                          errmsg("multiple ORDER BY clauses not allowed"),
   16134              :                                          parser_errposition(exprLocation((Node *) sortClause))));
   16135           18 :                 stmt->sortClause = sortClause;
   16136              :         }
   16137              :         /* We can handle multiple locking clauses, though */
   16138           30 :         stmt->lockingClause = list_concat(stmt->lockingClause, lockingClause);

   16229              : /* doNegate()
   16230              :  * Handle negation of a numeric constant.
   16231              :  *
   16232              :  * Formerly, we did this here because the optimizer couldn't cope with
   16233              :  * indexquals that looked like "var = -4" --- it wants "var = const"
   16234              :  * and a unary minus operator applied to a constant didn't qualify.
   16235              :  * As of Postgres 7.0, that problem doesn't exist anymore because there
   16236              :  * is a constant-subexpression simplifier in the optimizer.  However,
   16237              :  * there's still a good reason for doing this here, which is that we can
   16238              :  * postpone committing to a particular internal representation for simple

   16247           17 :                 A_Const *con = (A_Const *)n;
   16248              : 
   16249              :                 /* report the constant's location as that of the '-' sign */
   16250           17 :                 con->location = location;
   16251              : 
   16252           17 :                 if (con->val.type == T_Integer)
   16253              :                 {
   16254           17 :                         con->val.val.ival = -con->val.val.ival;
   16255           17 :                         return n;
   16256              :                 }
   16257            0 :                 if (con->val.type == T_Float)
   16258              :                 {
   16259            0 :                         doNegateFloat(&con->val);
   16260            0 :                         return n;
   16261              :                 }
   16262              :         }
   16263              : 
   16264            0 :         return (Node *) makeSimpleA_Expr(AEXPR_OP, "-", NULL, n, location);
   16265              : }
   16266              : 
   16267              : static void
   16268            0 : doNegateFloat(Value *v)
   16269              : {
   16270            0 :         char   *oldval = v->val.str;
   16271              : 
   16272              :         Assert(IsA(v, Float));
   16273            0 :         if (*oldval == '+')

   16275            0 :         if (*oldval == '-')
   16276            0 :                 v->val.str = oldval+1;       /* just strip the '-' */
   16277              :         else
   16278            0 :                 v->val.str = psprintf("-%s", oldval);
   16279            0 : }

   16572              : /*----------
   16573              :  * Recursive view transformation
   16574              :  *

   16576              :  *
   16577              :  *     CREATE RECURSIVE VIEW relname (aliases) AS query
   16578              :  *

   16581              :  *     CREATE VIEW relname (aliases) AS
   16582              :  *         WITH RECURSIVE relname (aliases) AS (query)

   16586              :  * view definition as the query.
   16587              :  * ----------
   16588              :  */
   16589              : static Node *
   16590            0 : makeRecursiveViewSelect(char *relname, List *aliases, Node *query)
